Concept: Support vector machines find the maximum-margin hyperplane; support vectors are the closest points; kernel trick maps data to higher dimensions for nonlinear separation.
Notes: SVM: max-margin hyperplane; support vectors = closest points; kernel trick → nonlinear via high-dim mapping

Concept: Principal component analysis (PCA): orthogonal transformation to uncorrelated components ordered by variance explained; dimensionality reduction preserving most variance.
Notes: PCA: orthogonal transform → uncorrelated components by variance; dimensionality reduction

Concept: Overfitting: model memorizes training data, fails to generalize; detected by train-val loss divergence; fixes: regularization, dropout, more data, early stopping.
Notes: overfitting: memorize train data → poor generalization; detect: train-val divergence; fix: regularization/dropout/data/early stop

Concept: Recurrent neural networks process sequences via hidden state h_t = f(W·h_{t-1} + U·x_t); suffer from vanishing gradients for long sequences; LSTMs address this.
Notes: RNN: h_t = f(W·h_{t-1}+U·x_t); sequential; vanishing gradient for long seqs; LSTM = fix

Concept: LSTM cells: input gate, forget gate, output gate, cell state; gates regulate information flow; cell state carries long-range memory; forget gate clears irrelevant info.
Notes: LSTM: input/forget/output gates + cell state; gates regulate flow; cell state = long-range memory

Concept: The encoder in a seq2seq model compresses input sequence into context vector (hidden state); single vector bottleneck limits performance for long sequences.
Notes: seq2seq encoder: input → context vector (hidden state); single-vector bottleneck = long-seq limitation

Concept: Attention mechanism solves the bottleneck: decoder attends to all encoder hidden states weighted by relevance; score = alignment function of decoder state + encoder state.
Notes: attention: decoder attends all encoder states; score = alignment(decoder state, encoder state); no bottleneck

Concept: Word2Vec: skip-gram predicts context from word; CBOW predicts word from context; learns dense word embeddings capturing semantic similarity.
Notes: word2vec: skip-gram = context from word; CBOW = word from context; learns semantic embeddings

Concept: GloVe embeddings: trained on word co-occurrence matrix; captures global statistical structure; performs well on analogy tasks (king − man + woman ≈ queen).
Notes: GloVe: co-occurrence matrix → embeddings; global stats; analogy: king−man+woman≈queen

Concept: BERT: bidirectional transformer pretrained on masked language modeling (MLM) and next sentence prediction (NSP); fine-tuned on downstream tasks.
Notes: BERT: bidirectional transformer; pretrain = MLM + NSP; fine-tune → downstream tasks

Concept: GPT architecture: decoder-only transformer; causal (left-to-right) attention; autoregressive pretraining on next-token prediction; few-shot prompting emergent at scale.
Notes: GPT: decoder-only; causal attention; pretrain = next-token prediction; few-shot prompting at scale

Concept: Tokenization strategies: character-level (simple, large seqs), word-level (large vocab, OOV), subword BPE/WordPiece (balance); most modern LLMs use BPE/tiktoken.
Notes: tokenization: char (simple/long), word (large vocab/OOV), subword BPE (balance); modern LLMs = BPE/tiktoken

Concept: The learning rate warmup prevents early training instability: during warmup, lr increases linearly from ε to target; transformers typically use 4000 warmup steps.
Notes: lr warmup: linear increase ε → target lr; prevents early instability; transformers ~4000 steps

Concept: Perplexity measures language model quality: PP = exp(avg cross-entropy loss); lower = better; equivalent to average branching factor of model predictions.
Notes: perplexity: PP = exp(avg CE loss); lower = better; avg branching factor of predictions

Concept: Data augmentation for NLP: back-translation, synonym replacement, random insertion/deletion/swap; increases effective dataset size and model robustness.
Notes: NLP data augmentation: back-translation, synonym swap, random insert/delete; ↑ dataset size + robustness

Concept: The encoder's multi-head self-attention allows every token to attend to every other token; enables capturing long-range dependencies in O(n²) time.
Notes: encoder self-attn: every token ↔ every other token; long-range dependencies; O(n²)

Concept: Seq2seq with teacher forcing: during training, feed ground-truth target tokens as decoder input (not model's previous output); faster convergence, exposure bias issue.
Notes: teacher forcing: feed ground-truth tokens to decoder during training; faster convergence; exposure bias trade-off

Concept: Hypothesis testing: null hypothesis H₀ (no effect); reject if p-value < α; Type I error = false rejection; Type II = false acceptance; power = 1 − β.
Notes: hypothesis testing: reject H₀ if p < α; Type I = false reject; Type II = false accept; power = 1−β

Concept: The bootstrap method: resample with replacement from dataset B times; compute statistic each time; distribution approximates sampling distribution; no distributional assumptions.
Notes: bootstrap: resample w/ replacement B times; compute stat → sampling distribution; no distributional assumption

Concept: Confidence intervals: a 95% CI means that if we repeated the experiment many times, 95% of intervals would contain the true parameter.
Notes: 95% CI: 95% of repeated-experiment intervals contain true parameter; NOT 95% prob parameter in interval

Concept: ANOVA (analysis of variance): tests whether means of 3+ groups differ; F-statistic = between-group variance / within-group variance; p < α → at least one group differs.
Notes: ANOVA: test 3+ group means; F = between-group var / within-group var; p<α → at least one group differs

Concept: Linear regression assumptions (GAUSS-MARKOV): linearity, independence, homoscedasticity, normality of residuals; violation → biased/inefficient estimates.
Notes: linear regression (Gauss-Markov): linearity + independence + homoscedasticity + normal residuals; violation → biased estimates

Concept: Regularized regression: Ridge adds L2 penalty → shrinks coefficients; Lasso adds L1 → sparse solution (feature selection); Elastic Net combines both.
Notes: regularized regression: Ridge (L2) = shrink; Lasso (L1) = sparse/select; Elastic Net = L1+L2

Concept: Logistic regression models probability via sigmoid: P(y=1|x) = 1/(1+e^(−w·x)); trained via maximum likelihood; log-odds linear in features.
Notes: logistic regression: P(y=1|x) = sigmoid(w·x); MLE training; log-odds linear in features

Concept: Survival analysis: models time-to-event data; Kaplan-Meier estimator; Cox proportional hazard model: h(t) = h₀(t)·exp(βx); hazard ratio interpretation.
Notes: survival analysis: time-to-event; Kaplan-Meier estimator; Cox: h(t)=h₀(t)·exp(βx); hazard ratio

Concept: Correlation ≠ causation: spurious correlations arise from confounders; randomized controlled trials (RCTs) establish causality by controlling confounders.
Notes: correlation ≠ causation; confounders create spurious correlations; RCTs establish causality via confounder control

Concept: The Bonferroni correction: for m simultaneous tests, use α/m as threshold; controls family-wise error rate; conservative when tests are correlated.
Notes: Bonferroni: multiple testing correction; use α/m; controls FWER; conservative w/ correlated tests

Concept: Alternative splicing allows one gene to produce multiple protein isoforms; regulated by exonic/intronic splicing enhancers/silencers and SR proteins.
Notes: alternative splicing: one gene → multiple isoforms; regulated by ESEs/ISEs/ESS/ISS + SR proteins

Concept: The unfolded protein response (UPR): ER stress (misfolded proteins) triggers ATF6, IRE1, PERK pathways; reduces protein synthesis, increases chaperones, triggers apoptosis if unresolved.
Notes: UPR: ER stress → ATF6/IRE1/PERK; ↓ translation, ↑ chaperones; unresolved → apoptosis

Concept: piRNA (Piwi-interacting RNA): small non-coding RNAs that silence transposons in germline cells; partner with PIWI proteins; protect genome integrity.
Notes: piRNA: silence transposons in germline; partner with PIWI proteins; protect genome integrity

Concept: The nucleosome: 147 bp DNA wrapped ~1.65× around histone octamer (H2A, H2B, H3, H4 ×2); ~10 nm fiber; H1 linker histone compacts into 30 nm fiber.
Notes: nucleosome: 147 bp DNA + histone octamer (H2A/H2B/H3/H4 ×2); H1 linker → 30 nm fiber

Concept: Chromatin remodeling complexes (SWI/SNF, NuRD) use ATP to reposition, eject, or restructure nucleosomes, regulating gene accessibility.
Notes: chromatin remodeling: SWI/SNF, NuRD; ATP-dependent repositioning/ejection of nucleosomes → regulate gene access

Concept: Liquid-liquid phase separation: intrinsically disordered regions of proteins form membraneless organelles (stress granules, nucleolus) via weak multivalent interactions.
Notes: LLPS: disordered protein regions → membraneless organelles (stress granules, nucleolus); weak multivalent interactions

Concept: m6A RNA methylation: most abundant internal mRNA modification; installed by METTL3/14 (writers), removed by FTO/ALKBH5 (erasers), read by YTHDF proteins.
Notes: m6A: most abundant mRNA modification; METTL3/14 = writers; FTO/ALKBH5 = erasers; YTHDF = readers

Concept: Cyclin-CDK complexes drive cell cycle progression: Cyclin D-CDK4/6 (G1), Cyclin E-CDK2 (G1/S), Cyclin A-CDK2 (S), Cyclin B-CDK1 (M); Rb phosphorylation = G1 checkpoint.
Notes: cyclin-CDK: D-CDK4/6 (G1) → E-CDK2 (G1/S) → A-CDK2 (S) → B-CDK1 (M); Rb-P = G1 checkpoint

Concept: The Notch pathway: Notch receptor cleaved by γ-secretase upon DLL/Jagged ligand binding → NICD enters nucleus → activates Hes/Hey target genes; regulates cell fate.
Notes: Notch: DLL/Jagged ligand → γ-secretase cleaves → NICD → nucleus → Hes/Hey transcription; cell fate

Concept: Ferroptosis: iron-dependent cell death via lipid peroxidation; distinct from apoptosis; driven by GPX4 inhibition; potential cancer therapy target.
Notes: ferroptosis: iron-dependent cell death; lipid peroxidation; GPX4 inhibition → death; distinct from apoptosis; cancer target

Concept: Cortical columns are vertical units of functionally related neurons; in V1, orientation columns respond to specific edge orientations; ocular dominance columns.
Notes: cortical columns: vertical functional units; V1 = orientation columns + ocular dominance columns

Concept: Default mode network (DMN): active at rest, deactivated during tasks; involved in self-referential thought, mind-wandering, autobiographical memory; nodes: mPFC, PCC, hippocampus.
Notes: DMN: active at rest, deactivated during tasks; self-reference/mind-wandering; mPFC + PCC + hippocampus

Concept: Adult neurogenesis occurs in the hippocampal dentate gyrus and olfactory bulb; new neurons integrate into circuits; impaired in depression, enhanced by exercise.
Notes: adult neurogenesis: dentate gyrus + olfactory bulb; impaired in depression; enhanced by exercise

Concept: Dopamine hypothesis of schizophrenia: excess D2 receptor activity in mesolimbic pathway → positive symptoms; hypodopaminergia in PFC → negative/cognitive symptoms.
Notes: schizophrenia DA hypothesis: excess D2 mesolimbic → positive symptoms; hypo-DA PFC → negative/cognitive symptoms

Concept: Synaptic plasticity rules: Hebbian (correlated activity → potentiation), STDP (spike timing dependent: pre-before-post → LTP; post-before-pre → LTD).
Notes: synaptic plasticity: Hebbian (correlated → potentiate); STDP: pre→post = LTP; post→pre = LTD

Concept: The basal ganglia direct pathway (D1 receptors): cortex → striatum → GPi/SNr inhibition → thalamus disinhibited → cortex activated; facilitates movement.
Notes: BG direct pathway: cortex → striatum (D1) → GPi/SNr inhibited → thalamus disinhibited → movement facilitated

Concept: Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) is produced by choroid plexus, circulates through ventricles and subarachnoid space, and drains via arachnoid villi into venous sinuses.
Notes: CSF: produced by choroid plexus; circulates ventricles + subarachnoid space; drains via arachnoid villi

Concept: The hypothalamus regulates: temperature (anterior = cooling, posterior = heating), hunger (lateral = hunger, ventromedial = satiety), thirst, circadian rhythms, endocrine output via pituitary.
Notes: hypothalamus: temp (anterior=cool, posterior=heat); hunger (lateral) + satiety (VMH); thirst; circadian; pituitary control

Concept: Myelination by oligodendrocytes (CNS) and Schwann cells (PNS) insulates axons; saltatory conduction jumps between Nodes of Ranvier; increases conduction velocity.
Notes: myelination: oligodendrocytes (CNS) + Schwann (PNS); saltatory conduction at Nodes of Ranvier; velocity ↑

Concept: Sleep stages: NREM stages 1-3 (deepening) + REM; slow-wave sleep (N3) crucial for declarative memory consolidation; REM for procedural/emotional memory.
Notes: sleep: NREM 1-3 + REM; N3 (slow-wave) = declarative memory; REM = procedural/emotional memory

Concept: Ricardian equivalence: consumers anticipate future taxes to pay for current deficits and increase savings, offsetting fiscal stimulus; disputed empirically.
Notes: Ricardian equivalence: consumers save for future taxes → offsets fiscal stimulus; empirically disputed

Concept: The natural rate of unemployment: consists of frictional (job searching) + structural (skill mismatch) unemployment; cyclical unemployment is the gap from natural rate.
Notes: natural unemployment: frictional (job search) + structural (skill mismatch); cyclical = gap from natural rate

Concept: Exchange rate determinants: purchasing power parity (PPP), interest rate differentials (UIP), current account balance, capital flows, and market sentiment.
Notes: exchange rate drivers: PPP + interest differentials (UIP) + current account + capital flows + sentiment

Concept: Central bank credibility and inflation targeting: if CB commits credibly to low inflation, inflation expectations anchor; reduces sacrifice ratio for disinflation.
Notes: CB credibility: credible low inflation target → anchored expectations → lower sacrifice ratio for disinflation

Concept: The Ramsey growth model: optimizing households choose consumption path to maximize lifetime utility; saddle-path equilibrium; golden rule of capital accumulation.
Notes: Ramsey model: households maximize lifetime utility; saddle-path equilibrium; golden rule of capital

Concept: Tobin's Q: ratio of market value of capital to its replacement cost; Q>1 → invest; Q<1 → don't invest; links financial markets to real investment.
Notes: Tobin's Q: market value / replacement cost; Q>1 → invest; Q<1 → don't; links finance ↔ real investment

Concept: The Balassa-Samuelson effect: productivity growth in tradable sector → wage ↑ → non-tradable prices ↑ → overall inflation higher in fast-growing economies.
Notes: Balassa-Samuelson: tradable productivity ↑ → wages ↑ → non-tradable prices ↑ → inflation ↑ in fast-growing economies

Concept: Signaling theory (Spence): workers invest in education not just for human capital but to signal ability to employers; equilibrium can be separating or pooling.
Notes: Spence signaling: education signals ability (not just human capital); separating vs. pooling equilibrium

Concept: The business judgment rule: courts defer to corporate directors' decisions if made in good faith, on an informed basis, and in the honest belief of acting in company's best interest.
Notes: business judgment rule: court defers to directors if good faith + informed + honest belief in company interest

Concept: Piercing the corporate veil: courts disregard corporate entity to hold shareholders liable when used as alter ego or to perpetuate fraud; requires undercapitalization + control.
Notes: pierce corporate veil: shareholder liable if corp = alter ego or fraud; requires undercapitalization + control

Concept: The Chevron doctrine: courts defer to agency interpretation of ambiguous statutes if the agency's interpretation is reasonable (step 1 = ambiguity; step 2 = reasonableness).
Notes: Chevron deference: step 1 = statute ambiguous?; step 2 = agency interpretation reasonable? → defer

Concept: The exclusionary rule: evidence obtained in violation of the Fourth Amendment is inadmissible; exceptions include good faith reliance on a warrant and inevitable discovery.
Notes: exclusionary rule: 4th Amend violation → evidence inadmissible; exceptions: good faith warrant + inevitable discovery

Concept: Promissory note vs. bond: promissory note is a personal promise to repay; bond is a formal debt instrument issued to multiple investors; both are negotiable instruments under UCC.
Notes: promissory note = personal repayment promise; bond = formal multi-investor debt; both = UCC negotiable instruments

Concept: Herd immunity threshold: T = 1 − 1/R₀; for measles (R₀≈15), ~93% population immunity needed; can be achieved via vaccination or natural infection.
Notes: herd immunity: T = 1−1/R₀; measles R₀≈15 → ~93% immunity needed; via vaccine or natural infection

Concept: Number needed to treat (NNT): reciprocal of absolute risk reduction; NNT=1 = perfect treatment; NNT=100 = treat 100 to prevent 1 event; compare vs. NNH.
Notes: NNT = 1/ARR; NNT=1 = ideal; NNT=100 → treat 100 to prevent 1 event; compare with NNH

Concept: The Bradford Hill criteria for causation: strength, consistency, specificity, temporality (required), biological gradient, plausibility, coherence, experiment, analogy.
Notes: Bradford Hill causation: strength + consistency + specificity + temporality (required) + gradient + plausibility + coherence

Concept: Sensitivity vs. specificity: sensitivity = TP/(TP+FN) = ability to detect disease; specificity = TN/(TN+FP) = ability to rule out; trade-off at different thresholds.
Notes: sensitivity = TP/(TP+FN) = detect disease; specificity = TN/(TN+FP) = rule out; trade-off by threshold

Concept: Recall bias in case-control studies: cases more likely to recall exposures than controls; leads to overestimation of association; minimized by objective exposure measurement.
Notes: recall bias: cases over-report exposure vs. controls → overestimates association; fix: objective exposure measurement

Concept: The nitrogen cycle: N₂ fixation (Rhizobium), nitrification (NH₄⁺ → NO₂⁻ → NO₃⁻), denitrification (NO₃⁻ → N₂), assimilation (plants); disrupted by synthetic fertilizers.
Notes: N cycle: N₂ fixation (Rhizobium) → nitrification → denitrification → N₂; synthetic fertilizers disrupt

Concept: Bioaccumulation: toxins accumulate in organisms faster than eliminated; biomagnification = concentration increases up trophic levels (DDT in birds of prey).
Notes: bioaccumulation: toxin accumulates faster than cleared; biomagnification = ↑ conc up trophic levels; DDT in raptors

Concept: The IPCC AR6 (2021): global surface temp 1.1°C above pre-industrial; ~1.5°C likely by 2030s; irreversible changes likely above 2°C; rapid emission cuts needed.
Notes: IPCC AR6 2021: +1.1°C above pre-industrial; ~1.5°C by 2030s; irreversible above 2°C; rapid cuts needed

Concept: Ecosystem services: provisioning (food, water), regulating (climate, pollination), cultural (recreation, aesthetic), supporting (nutrient cycling, soil formation).
Notes: ecosystem services: provisioning (food/water) + regulating (climate/pollination) + cultural (recreation) + supporting (nutrient cycling)

Concept: Carbon sequestration: forests, wetlands, soils, and ocean absorb CO₂; blue carbon (mangroves, seagrasses) especially efficient; threatened by deforestation.
Notes: carbon sequestration: forests/wetlands/soils/ocean absorb CO₂; blue carbon (mangroves/seagrasses) efficient; deforestation threat

Concept: The Byzantine fault tolerance problem: in a distributed system with n nodes, the system can tolerate up to ⌊(n−1)/3⌋ malicious (Byzantine) nodes.
Notes: Byzantine fault tolerance: n nodes tolerate ⌊(n−1)/3⌋ malicious nodes; e.g., n=4 tolerates 1

Concept: Consistent hashing: maps keys and nodes to a ring; key assigned to first node clockwise; adding/removing nodes only remaps adjacent keys; minimizes remapping.
Notes: consistent hashing: keys + nodes on ring; key → first clockwise node; add/remove → minimal remapping

Concept: The MapReduce paradigm: Map function processes input key-value pairs; Reduce aggregates values by key; enables distributed computation on large datasets.
Notes: MapReduce: Map = process key-value pairs; Reduce = aggregate by key; distributed large-scale computation

Concept: The OSI model has 7 layers: Physical, Data Link, Network (IP), Transport (TCP), Session, Presentation, Application; encapsulation adds headers at each layer.
Notes: OSI 7 layers: Physical → Data Link → Network (IP) → Transport (TCP) → Session → Presentation → Application

Concept: Bloom filters: probabilistic data structure; test set membership; false positives possible, false negatives not; uses k hash functions and bit array; space-efficient.
Notes: Bloom filter: probabilistic membership test; false positives possible, false negatives impossible; k hash functions + bit array

Concept: Mutex vs. semaphore: mutex = binary lock for mutual exclusion, owned by locking thread; semaphore = signaling mechanism, can be released by any thread; semaphores used for counting.
Notes: mutex: binary, owned by locking thread; semaphore: signaling, any thread can release; semaphore = counting

Concept: Garbage collection strategies: reference counting (Swift/Python), mark-and-sweep (JVM), generational GC (most modern runtimes); GC pause is latency trade-off.
Notes: GC: reference counting (Swift/Python), mark-and-sweep (JVM), generational (modern); GC pause = latency cost

Concept: Docker vs. Kubernetes: Docker packages and runs containers on a single host; Kubernetes orchestrates containers across a cluster (scheduling, scaling, self-healing).
Notes: Docker: run containers on single host; Kubernetes: orchestrate across cluster (schedule/scale/self-heal)

Concept: ACID properties for databases: Atomicity (all or nothing), Consistency (valid state), Isolation (concurrent transactions don't interfere), Durability (committed data persists).
Notes: ACID: Atomicity (all-or-nothing) + Consistency (valid) + Isolation (no interference) + Durability (persists)

Concept: The two-phase commit protocol (2PC): coordinator asks all nodes to prepare; if all agree, coordinator sends commit; if any fail, coordinator sends abort; blocking on coordinator failure.
Notes: 2PC: prepare phase → all agree → commit; any fail → abort; blocks on coordinator failure

Concept: The Flynn effect: average IQ scores rose ~3 points per decade throughout the 20th century; attributed to improved nutrition, education, and reduced disease burden.
Notes: Flynn effect: IQ ↑ ~3 pts/decade in 20th c.; causes: nutrition + education + ↓ disease

Concept: Replication crisis in science: many published findings (especially psychology, medicine) fail to replicate; causes: p-hacking, HARKing, publication bias, small samples.
Notes: replication crisis: findings fail to replicate; causes: p-hacking + HARKing + publication bias + small n

Concept: The Matthew effect in science: citations beget citations; highly cited papers accumulate more citations; early career researchers disadvantaged; winner-take-all dynamics.
Notes: Matthew effect in science: cited papers → more citations; early-career disadvantaged; winner-take-all

Concept: The CRISPR-Cas system has been adapted for diagnostics (SHERLOCK, DETECTR): guide RNA directs Cas13/Cas12 to target nucleic acid → reporter cleavage → detectable signal.
Notes: CRISPR diagnostics: SHERLOCK (Cas13)/DETECTR (Cas12); guide RNA → target → reporter cleavage → signal

Concept: The reproducibility spectrum: computational reproducibility (same code/data → same result), replicability (new data + same method), generalizability (new context).
Notes: reproducibility spectrum: computational (same code/data) → replicability (new data) → generalizability (new context)

Concept: Exosome biogenesis: multivesicular bodies (MVBs) form by inward budding; MVBs fuse with plasma membrane → exosomes released; carry proteins, miRNA, mRNA.
Notes: exosomes: MVB inward budding → plasma membrane fusion → release; carry proteins + miRNA + mRNA

Concept: The exposome: the totality of environmental exposures from conception to death; complements genome in explaining disease; includes diet, pollutants, stress, microbiome.
Notes: exposome: all environmental exposures conception→death; complements genome; diet + pollutants + stress + microbiome

Concept: Optogenetics: light-sensitive opsins (channelrhodopsin = activating; halorhodopsin = inhibiting) expressed in neurons; millisecond-precise control of neural circuits with light.
Notes: optogenetics: channelrhodopsin (activate) + halorhodopsin (inhibit) expressed in neurons; light → ms-precise circuit control

Concept: The placebo-nocebo asymmetry: positive expectation → therapeutic benefit (placebo); negative expectation → harm (nocebo); both mediated by learning and endogenous systems.
Notes: placebo: positive expectation → benefit; nocebo: negative expectation → harm; both = expectation + endogenous systems

Concept: The developmental origins of health and disease (DOHaD): fetal programming by maternal nutrition/stress shapes metabolic, immune, and neural outcomes in offspring.
Notes: DOHaD: fetal programming via maternal nutrition/stress → metabolic/immune/neural outcomes in offspring

Concept: The masked language model (MLM) objective: randomly mask 15% of tokens; model predicts masked tokens; enables bidirectional context learning (BERT).
Notes: MLM: mask 15% tokens → predict masked; enables bidirectional context; BERT pretraining

Concept: Subword tokenization reduces OOV problem: rare words split into known subwords; e.g., 'tokenization' → 'token', '##ization'; vocabulary typically 30K-50K tokens.
Notes: subword tokenization: rare words → subwords; eliminates OOV; vocab 30K-50K; e.g., 'token'+'##ization'

Concept: Temperature in text generation: divides logits before softmax; T>1 = more random/creative; T<1 = more deterministic/focused; T→0 = argmax (greedy).
Notes: temperature: logits/T before softmax; T>1 = random; T<1 = deterministic; T→0 = greedy/argmax

Concept: Top-k sampling: keep only top k tokens at each step; top-p (nucleus) sampling: keep smallest set whose cumulative prob ≥ p; top-p adapts vocabulary size dynamically.
Notes: top-k: keep k tokens/step; top-p (nucleus): cumulative prob ≥ p; top-p = dynamic k; both prevent low-prob sampling

Concept: The cross-attention sublayer in the decoder: Q comes from decoder, K and V come from encoder output; allows decoder to selectively attend to relevant encoder states.
Notes: cross-attention: Q=decoder, K/V=encoder output; decoder selectively attends encoder; core seq2seq mechanism

Concept: Layer normalization: normalizes across feature dimension (not batch); stable for small batches; Pre-LN (before attention) more stable than Post-LN during training.
Notes: layer norm: normalize across features; stable small batches; Pre-LN (before attn) = more stable than Post-LN

Concept: Sparse attention (Longformer, BigBird): combines local window attention + global tokens; reduces O(n²) to O(n); handles documents up to 4096+ tokens.
Notes: sparse attention: local window + global tokens; O(n²) → O(n); handles 4096+ tokens; Longformer/BigBird

Concept: The beam search length penalty: lp(Y) = ((5+|Y|)/(5+1))^α; penalizes short sequences when α>0; prevents beam search from favoring shorter outputs.
Notes: beam search length penalty: lp = ((5+|Y|)/6)^α; α>0 penalizes short sequences; prevents short-output bias

Concept: Knowledge distillation: student model trained to match teacher's soft probability outputs (logits) rather than hard labels; smaller model inherits knowledge.
Notes: knowledge distillation: student matches teacher's soft outputs (logits); inherits knowledge; smaller/faster model

Concept: Positional embeddings: sinusoidal (fixed, handles arbitrary lengths), learned (better on training distribution), rotary (RoPE, relative position, used in LLaMA).
Notes: positional embeddings: sinusoidal (fixed/any length), learned (train-dist), rotary RoPE (relative, LLaMA)

Concept: The cerebral cortex is organized into four lobes: frontal (executive, motor), parietal (somatosensory, spatial), temporal (auditory, memory), occipital (visual).
Notes: cortex: frontal (executive/motor) + parietal (somatosensory/spatial) + temporal (auditory/memory) + occipital (visual)

Concept: Glutamate is the primary excitatory neurotransmitter; receptors: AMPA (fast), NMDA (Ca²⁺, plasticity), mGluR (modulatory); excess = excitotoxicity.
Notes: glutamate: primary excitatory NT; AMPA (fast) + NMDA (Ca²⁺/plasticity) + mGluR (modulatory); excess → excitotoxicity

Concept: GABA is the primary inhibitory neurotransmitter; GABA_A (Cl⁻ channel, fast) and GABA_B (G-protein, slow); benzodiazepines enhance GABA_A.
Notes: GABA: primary inhibitory NT; GABA_A = Cl⁻ channel (fast); GABA_B = G-protein (slow); benzos ↑ GABA_A

Concept: Serotonin (5-HT) modulates mood, appetite, sleep; synthesized from tryptophan; SSRIs block reuptake; 5-HT_3 receptor = ligand-gated ion channel (others = GPCRs).
Notes: serotonin (5-HT): mood/appetite/sleep; from tryptophan; SSRIs block reuptake; 5-HT_3 = LGIC; others = GPCR

Concept: The autonomic nervous system: sympathetic (fight-or-flight, thoracolumbar, NE) vs. parasympathetic (rest-and-digest, craniosacral, ACh); dual innervation of organs.
Notes: ANS: sympathetic (fight-or-flight, thoracolumbar, NE) vs. parasympathetic (rest-digest, craniosacral, ACh)

Concept: Protein secondary structure: α-helix (H-bond every 4 residues, ~3.6 aa/turn) and β-sheet (parallel or antiparallel H-bonds); determined by Ramachandran plot.
Notes: protein 2° structure: α-helix (H-bond every 4 res, 3.6 aa/turn); β-sheet (parallel/antiparallel); Ramachandran plot

Concept: Passive transport: diffusion or facilitated diffusion down concentration gradient; no energy required; rate determined by Fick's law.
Notes: passive transport: diffusion (or facilitated) down gradient; no ATP; rate = Fick's law

Concept: Active transport: moves solutes against gradient; requires ATP; primary (direct ATP, Na/K-ATPase) vs. secondary (uses gradient created by primary, SGLT).
Notes: active transport: against gradient + ATP; primary = direct ATP (Na/K-ATPase); secondary = uses gradient (SGLT)

Concept: The extracellular matrix (ECM): collagen (structural), fibronectin (adhesion), laminin (basement membrane), proteoglycans; scaffold for cells; signaling platform.
Notes: ECM: collagen (structure) + fibronectin (adhesion) + laminin (basement membrane) + proteoglycans; cell scaffold + signaling

Concept: Tight junctions seal adjacent epithelial cells; claudins and occludins form strands; regulate paracellular permeability; form blood-brain barrier, gut epithelium.
Notes: tight junctions: claudins + occludins; seal epithelial cells; regulate paracellular flux; BBB + gut barrier

Concept: Gap junctions: connexin hexamers form connexons; pairs connect adjacent cells; allow ions and small molecules (<1 kDa) to pass; electrical coupling of cardiomyocytes.
Notes: gap junctions: connexin hexamers = connexons; adjacent cell coupling; ions + small molecules <1 kDa; cardiac electrical coupling

Concept: Fatty acid β-oxidation: occurs in mitochondrial matrix; each cycle removes 2C as acetyl-CoA; produces NADH, FADH₂; 16C palmitate yields 129 ATP net.
Notes: β-oxidation: mitochondrial matrix; each cycle: −2C → acetyl-CoA + NADH + FADH₂; palmitate (16C) → 129 ATP net

Concept: Gluconeogenesis: synthesis of glucose from non-carbohydrate precursors (lactate, amino acids, glycerol); occurs mainly in liver; bypasses irreversible glycolytic steps.
Notes: gluconeogenesis: glucose from lactate/AAs/glycerol; mainly liver; bypasses irreversible glycolysis steps

Concept: The coagulation cascade: intrinsic (XII) and extrinsic (TF-VIIa) pathways converge at factor X; thrombin converts fibrinogen → fibrin clot; amplified by factors V, VIII.
Notes: coagulation: intrinsic (XII) + extrinsic (TF-VIIa) → factor X → thrombin → fibrinogen → fibrin; amplified by V/VIII

Concept: Monoclonal antibodies: produced by hybridoma (myeloma + B cell fusion); single epitope specificity; therapeutic applications: rituximab (CD20), trastuzumab (HER2), pembrolizumab (PD-1).
Notes: monoclonal Ab: hybridoma = myeloma + B cell; single epitope; examples: rituximab (CD20), trastuzumab (HER2), pembrolizumab (PD-1)

Concept: The renin-angiotensin system is targeted by ACE inhibitors (block Ang II formation) and ARBs (block AT1 receptor); used in hypertension, heart failure, CKD.
Notes: RAAS targets: ACE inhibitors (block Ang II) + ARBs (block AT1R); indications: HTN, HF, CKD

Concept: Cardiac output = heart rate × stroke volume; stroke volume depends on preload (end-diastolic volume), afterload (resistance), and contractility (Frank-Starling).
Notes: CO = HR × SV; SV depends on preload (EDV) + afterload (resistance) + contractility (Frank-Starling)

Concept: The ventilation-perfusion (V/Q) ratio: normal ~0.8; V/Q=0 (shunt, perfused but not ventilated); V/Q=∞ (dead space, ventilated but not perfused); both cause hypoxemia.
Notes: V/Q: normal ~0.8; V/Q=0 = shunt (perfused/no vent); V/Q=∞ = dead space (vent/no perf); both → hypoxemia

Concept: The HLA system (MHC in humans): HLA-A, B, C are class I; HLA-DR, DP, DQ are class II; highly polymorphic; determines organ transplant compatibility; associated with autoimmune disease.
Notes: HLA: class I = A/B/C (all nucleated cells); class II = DR/DP/DQ (APCs); polymorphic; transplant compatibility + autoimmune disease

Concept: Autophagy: cellular self-digestion; macroautophagy = double-membrane autophagosome engulfs cargo → lysosome fusion; mTOR suppresses, starvation activates.
Notes: autophagy: double-membrane autophagosome → lysosome; mTOR suppresses; starvation activates; macro/micro/chaperone-mediated forms

Concept: The MAPK/ERK pathway: growth factor → RTK → RAS → RAF → MEK → ERK → nucleus → proliferation gene transcription; dysregulated in many cancers (BRAF V600E).
Notes: MAPK/ERK: growth factor → RTK → RAS → RAF → MEK → ERK → proliferation; BRAF V600E = cancer mutation

Concept: The PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway: PI3K phosphorylates PIP2 → PIP3 → AKT activation → mTOR → protein synthesis + cell survival; frequently activated in cancer.
Notes: PI3K-AKT-mTOR: PI3K → PIP3 → AKT → mTOR → protein synthesis + survival; frequently hyperactivated in cancer

Concept: Base excision repair (BER): glycosylase removes damaged base → AP endonuclease nicks backbone → polymerase fills → ligase seals; handles oxidative damage.
Notes: BER: glycosylase removes base → AP endonuclease → polymerase fills → ligase; handles oxidative damage

Concept: Nucleotide excision repair (NER): recognizes bulky lesions (UV dimers); ~30 proteins; excises ~30 nt patch; global-genome NER + transcription-coupled NER.
Notes: NER: bulky lesions (UV dimers); ~30 proteins; excise ~30 nt patch; GG-NER + TC-NER

Concept: The spindle assembly checkpoint (SAC): BubR1/Mad2 inhibit APC/C until all kinetochores attached to spindle; prevents premature chromatid separation.
Notes: SAC: BubR1/Mad2 inhibit APC/C until all kinetochores attached; prevents premature sister chromatid separation

Concept: Transcription factors bind DNA via structural motifs: zinc finger, leucine zipper (bZIP), helix-turn-helix (HTH), helix-loop-helix (HLH); often dimerize for binding.
Notes: TF DNA-binding motifs: zinc finger, bZIP (leucine zipper), HTH, HLH; often dimerize to bind DNA

Concept: The Src family kinases: non-receptor tyrosine kinases regulated by phosphorylation at activating Y416 and inhibitory Y527; involved in growth, adhesion, immune signaling.
Notes: Src kinases: non-receptor TK; activating Y416, inhibitory Y527; growth + adhesion + immune signaling

Concept: The tumor microenvironment (TME): cancer cells, stromal cells, immune cells, ECM, and vasculature interact; immunosuppressive TME (Tregs, M2 macrophages, PD-L1) enables immune escape.
Notes: TME: cancer + stroma + immune + ECM + vasculature; immunosuppressive (Tregs/M2/PD-L1) → immune escape

Concept: Chimeric antigen receptor T cells (CAR-T): T cells engineered with synthetic receptor targeting tumor antigens (CD19); effective in B-cell malignancies; toxicities: CRS, neurotoxicity.
Notes: CAR-T: engineered T cells with synthetic tumor receptor (e.g., CD19); effective B-cell malignancies; CRS + neurotoxicity

Concept: Checkpoint inhibitors: anti-PD-1 (nivolumab, pembrolizumab), anti-PD-L1 (atezolizumab), anti-CTLA4 (ipilimumab); block coinhibitory signals → T cell activation; immune-related AEs.
Notes: checkpoint inhibitors: anti-PD-1 (nivo/pembro), anti-PD-L1 (atezolizumab), anti-CTLA4 (ipilimumab); block coinhibitory → T cell activation; irAEs

Concept: The complement lectin pathway: mannose-binding lectin (MBL) binds carbohydrates on pathogens → MASP1/2 activation → C4/C2/C3 cleavage → MAC.
Notes: complement lectin: MBL binds pathogen carbs → MASP1/2 → C4/C2 → C3 → MAC

Concept: Positive selection in thymus: T cells that recognize self-MHC survive; those that don't bind die by neglect; ~5% survive; cortical thymic epithelial cells present MHC.
Notes: thymic positive selection: T cells recognize self-MHC → survive; ~5% pass; cortical TECs present MHC

Concept: Negative selection in thymus: T cells with too-high affinity for self-peptide/MHC are deleted (central tolerance); eliminates potentially autoreactive clones.
Notes: thymic negative selection: high-affinity self-peptide/MHC → clonal deletion; central tolerance; medullary TECs

Concept: Class switching (CSR): B cells switch antibody isotype (IgM → IgG/IgA/IgE) via recombination at switch regions; requires CD40L-CD40 + cytokine signals; AID enzyme.
Notes: CSR: IgM → IgG/IgA/IgE; recombination at switch regions; CD40L + cytokines + AID enzyme

Concept: Somatic hypermutation: AID introduces point mutations in V regions of Ig genes in germinal centers; affinity maturation selects high-affinity B cells.
Notes: somatic hypermutation: AID mutates Ig V regions in GCs; affinity maturation selects high-affinity B cells

Concept: The inflammasome: cytoplasmic multiprotein complex (NLRP3 most studied); senses danger signals → caspase-1 activation → IL-1β/IL-18 maturation + pyroptosis.
Notes: inflammasome: NLRP3 + caspase-1 → IL-1β/IL-18 maturation + pyroptosis; senses danger signals

Concept: Neutrophil extracellular traps (NETs): neutrophils release chromatin + antimicrobial proteins to trap pathogens; NET-osis is a form of cell death; contributes to thrombosis.
Notes: NETs: neutrophils release chromatin + antimicrobial proteins; trap pathogens; NET-osis; contributes to thrombosis

Concept: The mTOR complex: mTORC1 (rapamycin-sensitive) promotes anabolism, senses nutrients/growth factors; mTORC2 regulates AKT + actin cytoskeleton.
Notes: mTOR: mTORC1 (rapamycin-sensitive) = anabolism + nutrient sensor; mTORC2 = AKT regulation + actin

Concept: Warburg effect mechanism: cancer upregulates GLUT transporters and hexokinase; lactate fermentation despite O₂ supports rapid biomass synthesis (pentose phosphate, lipids).
Notes: Warburg mechanism: ↑ GLUT + hexokinase; aerobic glycolysis → lactate; supports biomass (pentose phosphate + lipid synthesis)

Concept: The protein synthesis machinery: ribosomes (80S eukaryotic = 60S + 40S); initiation (cap-dependent, eIF4E); elongation (EF-1α delivers aa-tRNA); termination (eRF1/3).
Notes: protein synthesis: 80S ribosome (60S+40S); initiation = cap + eIF4E; elongation = EF-1α delivers aa-tRNA; termination = eRF1/3

Concept: Telomere shortening in somatic cells: lack of telomerase → replicative senescence after ~50 divisions (Hayflick limit); bypassed in cancer by telomerase reactivation or ALT.
Notes: telomere shortening: no telomerase → senescence after ~50 divisions (Hayflick); cancer bypasses via telomerase or ALT

Concept: The integrated stress response (ISR): four kinases (HRI, GCN2, PKR, PERK) phosphorylate eIF2α → global translation inhibition + ATF4 induction; stress-adaptive.
Notes: ISR: HRI/GCN2/PKR/PERK → eIF2α-P → global translation ↓ + ATF4 ↑; stress adaptation

Concept: Cancer hallmarks (Hanahan & Weinberg): self-sufficiency in growth signals, evading apoptosis, limitless replication, sustained angiogenesis, invasion/metastasis, resisting growth inhibitors.
Notes: cancer hallmarks: self-sufficient growth + evade apoptosis + limitless replication + angiogenesis + metastasis + resist growth inhibition

Concept: The nuclear export signal (NES) and nuclear localization signal (NLS): NLS recognized by importins → nucleus; NES recognized by exportins (CRM1/XPO1) → cytoplasm.
Notes: NLS → importins → nucleus; NES → exportins (CRM1) → cytoplasm; regulate protein localization

Concept: Lipid rafts: cholesterol- and sphingolipid-enriched membrane microdomains; more ordered than surrounding bilayer; concentrate signaling molecules (GPI-anchored proteins, Src).
Notes: lipid rafts: cholesterol + sphingolipid microdomains; ordered phase; concentrate signaling proteins (GPI-anchored, Src)

Concept: Membrane potential: determined by relative permeability of ions (K⁺, Na⁺, Cl⁻) via Goldman equation; resting potential ~−70 mV in neurons dominated by K⁺ permeability.
Notes: membrane potential: Goldman equation; resting ~−70 mV; dominated by K⁺ permeability in neurons

Concept: Phagocytosis: receptor-mediated engulfment (FcγR, CR3) of large particles → phagosome → lysosome fusion → phagolysosome → degradation; key in macrophages/neutrophils.
Notes: phagocytosis: FcγR/CR3 → phagosome → phagolysosome → degradation; macrophages + neutrophils

Concept: Exocytosis: vesicle fusion with plasma membrane releases cargo; regulated (Ca²⁺-triggered, synaptic vesicles) vs. constitutive (continuous); SNARE proteins mediate fusion.
Notes: exocytosis: vesicle + PM fusion → cargo release; regulated (Ca²⁺-triggered) vs. constitutive; SNARE-mediated

Concept: G-protein cycle: Gα·GDP (inactive) + Gβγ; agonist-GPCR activates GEF activity → GTP exchange → Gα·GTP active → effector; intrinsic GTPase → Gα·GDP (inactivation).
Notes: G-protein cycle: Gα·GDP inactive; GPCR → GTP exchange → Gα·GTP active → effector; intrinsic GTPase → back to GDP

Concept: Second messengers: cAMP (adenylyl cyclase, activates PKA), cGMP (guanylyl cyclase, PKG), IP3 (PLC, releases ER Ca²⁺), DAG (activates PKC), Ca²⁺ (calmodulin).
Notes: 2nd messengers: cAMP (AC→PKA), cGMP (GC→PKG), IP3 (PLC→Ca²⁺), DAG (PKC), Ca²⁺ (calmodulin)

Concept: Receptor downregulation: prolonged agonist exposure → receptor phosphorylation by GRKs → β-arrestin recruitment → internalization → lysosomal degradation or recycling.
Notes: receptor downregulation: GRK phosphorylation → β-arrestin → internalization → degrade or recycle

Concept: The retina: photoreceptors (rods = dim light, cones = color); signal → bipolar → ganglion cells → optic nerve; center-surround receptive fields; fovea = high acuity.
Notes: retina: rods (dim) + cones (color) → bipolar → ganglion → optic nerve; center-surround RFs; fovea = high acuity

Concept: Olfactory transduction: odorant → GPCR → Golf → adenylyl cyclase III → cAMP → CNG channel → Ca²⁺/Na⁺ influx → depolarization; one neuron one receptor rule.
Notes: olfaction: odorant → GPCR → Golf → AC-III → cAMP → CNG channel → depolarization; 1 neuron = 1 receptor

Concept: The stretch reflex (myotatic): muscle stretch → Ia afferent → spinal cord → α-motor neuron → muscle contraction; monosynaptic; knee-jerk reflex example.
Notes: stretch reflex: stretch → Ia afferent → spinal α-motor neuron → contraction; monosynaptic; knee jerk

Concept: Renshaw cell inhibition: α-motor neuron collateral → Renshaw cell → releases glycine → inhibits same α-motor neuron; recurrent inhibition; limits motoneuron firing.
Notes: Renshaw inhibition: motor neuron collateral → Renshaw cell → glycine → inhibits motor neuron; recurrent inhibition

Concept: The corticospinal tract (pyramidal): from primary motor cortex → descends, mostly crosses in medullary pyramids → lateral corticospinal tract → α-motor neurons; voluntary movement.
Notes: corticospinal tract: M1 → medullary pyramids (cross) → lateral CST → α-motor neurons; voluntary movement

Concept: Mirror neurons in primates: fire both when performing and observing an action; location: F5 area (premotor cortex) and inferior parietal lobule; basis of action understanding.
Notes: mirror neurons: fire doing + observing same action; premotor F5 + inferior parietal; action understanding

Concept: The vestibulocochlear nerve (CN VIII): cochlear branch (hearing) and vestibular branch (balance); hair cells transduce sound/head movement via stereocilia mechanotransduction.
Notes: CN VIII: cochlear (hearing) + vestibular (balance); hair cell stereocilia mechanotransduction

Concept: The sodium-potassium ATPase pump moves three sodium ions out and two potassium ions in per ATP molecule hydrolyzed, maintaining resting membrane potential.
Notes: Na+/K+ ATPase: 3 Na+ out, 2 K+ in per ATP; maintains resting membrane potential

Concept: Tight junctions between epithelial cells prevent paracellular movement of solutes and form selective barriers such as the blood-brain barrier.
Notes: tight junctions → prevent paracellular solute movement; selective barrier; e.g., BBB

Concept: Receptor tyrosine kinases dimerize upon ligand binding, autophosphorylate tyrosine residues, and activate downstream signaling cascades including RAS-MAPK.
Notes: RTK: ligand → dimerization → autophosphorylation → RAS-MAPK cascade

Concept: The Krebs cycle generates two ATP, six NADH, and two FADH2 per glucose through two turns occurring in the mitochondrial matrix.
Notes: Krebs: 2 turns/glucose → 2 ATP + 6 NADH + 2 FADH2; mitochondrial matrix

Concept: Alternative splicing of pre-mRNA produces multiple protein isoforms from a single gene by including or excluding different exon combinations.
Notes: alt splicing → 1 gene → multiple protein isoforms; exon inclusion/exclusion

Concept: The lac operon is repressed when glucose is abundant via catabolite repression through low cAMP levels that fail to activate CAP.
Notes: lac operon repression: ↑ glucose → ↓ cAMP → CAP inactive → repression; catabolite repression

Concept: CRISPR base editing converts single nucleotides without double-strand breaks using deaminase enzymes; C-to-T or A-to-G changes possible.
Notes: CRISPR base editing: single-nuc change w/o DSB; deaminase → C→T or A→G; no cuts

Concept: The spindle assembly checkpoint prevents premature anaphase onset by having BubR1 and Mad2 inhibit APC/C until all kinetochores are attached.
Notes: SAC: BubR1 + Mad2 → inhibit APC/C; waits until all kinetochores attached; prevents premature anaphase

Concept: Negative feedback in the HPA axis: cortisol produced by the adrenal cortex inhibits both CRH release from the hypothalamus and ACTH from the pituitary.
Notes: HPA negative feedback: cortisol → ↓ CRH (hypothalamus) + ↓ ACTH (pituitary)

Concept: Ubiquitin chains tagged to substrate proteins mark them for degradation by the 26S proteasome, regulating cell cycle progression and protein quality control.
Notes: ubiquitin-proteasome: ubiquitin chain on substrate → 26S proteasome degrades; cell cycle + QC regulation

Concept: Fatty acid beta-oxidation removes two carbons per cycle as acetyl-CoA while producing one NADH and one FADH2; occurs in the mitochondrial matrix.
Notes: beta-oxidation: each cycle → -2C as acetyl-CoA + NADH + FADH2; mitochondrial matrix

Concept: The nucleosome consists of 147 base pairs of DNA wrapped 1.65 times around a histone octamer composed of H2A, H2B, H3, and H4 pairs.
Notes: nucleosome: 147 bp DNA × 1.65 wraps around octamer (H2A/H2B/H3/H4 ×2)

Concept: Wnt signaling stabilizes beta-catenin by inhibiting the destruction complex (APC, axin, GSK-3beta), allowing nuclear translocation and target gene activation.
Notes: Wnt: inhibits destruction complex (APC/axin/GSK-3β) → β-catenin stable → nucleus → target genes

Concept: Ferroptosis is iron-dependent regulated cell death driven by lipid peroxidation; GPX4 normally suppresses it using glutathione as cofactor.
Notes: ferroptosis: iron-dependent; lipid peroxidation → death; GPX4 + glutathione suppress normally

Concept: The complement lectin pathway activates when mannose-binding lectin recognizes carbohydrate patterns on pathogens, triggering MASP1/2-mediated cleavage of C4 and C2.
Notes: lectin complement: MBL binds pathogen carbs → MASP1/2 → cleaves C4 + C2 → cascade

Concept: Somatic hypermutation introduces point mutations in immunoglobulin variable regions within germinal centers, driven by AID enzyme to enable affinity maturation.
Notes: somatic hypermutation: AID → point mutations in Ig V-regions; germinal centers; → affinity maturation

Concept: The mTORC1 complex senses nutrients and growth factors to promote anabolic processes; rapamycin inhibits mTORC1 by binding FKBP12.
Notes: mTORC1: senses nutrients + GFs → anabolism; rapamycin + FKBP12 → inhibits mTORC1

Concept: G-protein coupled receptors activate when agonist binds, exchanging GDP for GTP on Galpha; intrinsic GTPase activity terminates signaling.
Notes: GPCR: agonist → GDP→GTP on Gα → active; intrinsic GTPase → GTP→GDP → terminates signal

Concept: Membrane lipid rafts are cholesterol- and sphingolipid-enriched microdomains in a liquid-ordered phase that concentrate GPI-anchored proteins and Src family kinases.
Notes: lipid rafts: cholesterol + sphingolipid → liquid-ordered phase; concentrate GPI-anchored proteins + Src kinases

Concept: DNA methylation at CpG islands by DNMT3A/3B silences gene expression; DNMT1 maintains methylation patterns during replication.
Notes: CpG methylation: DNMT3A/3B = writers (de novo); DNMT1 = maintenance during replication → gene silencing

Concept: The NLRP3 inflammasome senses danger signals, activates caspase-1, which cleaves pro-IL-1beta and pro-IL-18 to their active forms and triggers pyroptosis.
Notes: NLRP3 inflammasome: danger signal → caspase-1 → cleaves pro-IL-1β/IL-18 → pyroptosis

Concept: Exocytosis requires SNARE protein complex assembly between vesicle SNAREs (v-SNAREs) and target membrane SNAREs (t-SNAREs); Ca2+ triggers fusion.
Notes: exocytosis: v-SNARE + t-SNARE complex → Ca2+-triggered membrane fusion → cargo release

Concept: Class switch recombination allows B cells to change from IgM to IgG, IgA, or IgE production; requires AID enzyme and CD40-CD40L interaction.
Notes: CSR: IgM → IgG/IgA/IgE; requires AID + CD40-CD40L signaling; germinal centers

Concept: Cholesterol biosynthesis is controlled by SREBP transcription factors activated by low cellular cholesterol; statins inhibit HMG-CoA reductase, the rate-limiting enzyme.
Notes: cholesterol synthesis: low cholesterol → SREBP activated → gene expression ↑; statins inhibit HMG-CoA reductase (rate-limiting)

Concept: Homologous recombination repair uses sister chromatid as a template during S/G2 phases; requires BRCA1/2 for strand invasion and high-fidelity repair.
Notes: HR repair: S/G2 phases; sister chromatid template; BRCA1/2 required; high fidelity

Concept: The JAK-STAT pathway transmits cytokine signals: cytokine → receptor → JAK phosphorylates STAT → STAT dimerizes → nucleus → transcription.
Notes: JAK-STAT: cytokine → receptor → JAK → STAT-P → STAT dimer → nucleus → transcription

Concept: Lysosomal storage diseases result from deficient hydrolases that cause substrate accumulation; examples include Gaucher (glucocerebrosidase), Tay-Sachs (hexosaminidase A).
Notes: lysosomal storage diseases: deficient hydrolase → substrate accumulation; Gaucher (glucocerebrosidase), Tay-Sachs (hex-A)

Concept: Axonal transport uses kinesin motors for anterograde movement toward the synapse along microtubule plus-ends; dynein drives retrograde movement toward the cell body.
Notes: axonal transport: kinesin = anterograde (+end → synapse); dynein = retrograde (−end → soma)

Concept: The citric acid cycle is amphibolic, serving both catabolism for energy and anabolism by providing biosynthetic precursors such as oxaloacetate and alpha-ketoglutarate.
Notes: TCA = amphibolic: catabolism (energy) + anabolism (precursors: OAA, α-KG)

Concept: Voltage-gated calcium channels open at presynaptic terminals during action potentials; local Ca2+ increase triggers synaptic vesicle fusion and neurotransmitter release.
Notes: presynaptic: AP → VGCC opens → ↑ Ca2+ → vesicle fusion → NT release

Concept: The integrated stress response phosphorylates eIF2alpha via four kinases (HRI, GCN2, PKR, PERK) in response to different stresses, inhibiting global translation.
Notes: ISR: HRI/GCN2/PKR/PERK → eIF2α-P → ↓ global translation; stress-specific kinases

Concept: Chromatin remodeling complexes such as SWI/SNF use ATP hydrolysis to reposition or eject nucleosomes, regulating transcription factor access to DNA.
Notes: chromatin remodeling: SWI/SNF; ATP → reposition/eject nucleosomes → ↑ TF access to DNA

Concept: Autophagy: damaged organelles engulfed by double-membrane autophagosome → fuses with lysosome → phagolysosome → hydrolytic degradation; mTOR suppresses initiation.
Notes: autophagy: autophagosome (double membrane) engulfs cargo → lysosome fusion → degradation; mTOR suppresses

Concept: Myelin sheath allows saltatory conduction: action potential jumps between nodes of Ranvier, dramatically increasing conduction velocity with less energy.
Notes: myelin → saltatory conduction; AP jumps between Nodes of Ranvier; ↑ velocity + ↓ energy cost

Concept: The nuclear pore complex selectively controls nucleocytoplasmic transport; importins carry NLS-bearing proteins into nucleus; exportins carry NES-bearing proteins out.
Notes: NPC: importins carry NLS cargo → nucleus; exportins carry NES cargo → cytoplasm

Concept: Reactive oxygen species damage lipids, proteins, and DNA; superoxide dismutase converts O2•- to H2O2; catalase converts H2O2 to water.
Notes: ROS damage: lipids/proteins/DNA; SOD: O2•- → H2O2; catalase: H2O2 → H2O

Concept: PCR amplification: denaturation at 95°C separates strands; annealing at 55-65°C binds primers; extension at 72°C with Taq polymerase synthesizes new strand.
Notes: PCR: 95°C denature → 55-65°C anneal primers → 72°C Taq extension; exponential amplification

Concept: Telomeres shorten ~50-100 bp per cell division; Hayflick limit (~50 divisions) leads to replicative senescence; telomerase active in germline and cancer cells.
Notes: telomere: ↓ ~50-100 bp/division; Hayflick limit ~50 div → senescence; telomerase in germline + cancer

Concept: Glycolysis converts one glucose to two pyruvate, yielding 2 ATP and 2 NADH; occurs in cytoplasm; key regulated enzyme = phosphofructokinase-1 (PFK-1).
Notes: glycolysis: glucose → 2 pyruvate + 2 ATP + 2 NADH; cytoplasm; rate-limiting: PFK-1

Concept: Exosomes are 30-150 nm extracellular vesicles derived from multivesicular body fusion with plasma membrane; carry miRNA, mRNA, proteins for intercellular communication.
Notes: exosomes: 30-150 nm vesicles; MVB → PM fusion → release; cargo = miRNA/mRNA/proteins; cell-cell comm

Concept: The unfolded protein response activates three sensors (ATF6, IRE1, PERK) during ER stress to reduce protein load and increase chaperone expression.
Notes: UPR: ER stress → ATF6/IRE1/PERK; ↓ protein load + ↑ chaperones; unresolved → apoptosis

Concept: Restriction enzymes cut DNA at specific palindromic sequences; EcoRI recognizes GAATTC; used in cloning, Southern blotting, restriction mapping.
Notes: restriction enzymes: cut palindromic seqs; EcoRI = GAATTC; tools: cloning/Southern blot/restriction mapping

Concept: Western blot: SDS-PAGE separates proteins by MW → transfer to membrane → probe w/ 1° antibody → secondary Ab w/ HRP → chemiluminescent detection.
Notes: western blot: SDS-PAGE (MW sep) → membrane → 1° Ab → 2° Ab-HRP → chemiluminescence detection

Concept: The Warburg effect: cancer cells preferentially use aerobic glycolysis despite O2 availability; ↑ GLUT transporters + hexokinase; supports rapid biosynthesis.
Notes: Warburg: cancer → aerobic glycolysis despite O2; ↑ GLUT/hexokinase; biomass synthesis > ATP efficiency

Concept: Type IV hypersensitivity is T-cell mediated, not antibody-dependent; onset 48-72 hr; examples = tuberculin skin test, contact dermatitis, transplant rejection.
Notes: Type IV hypersensitivity: T-cell mediated; 48-72 hr delay; e.g., TB skin test, contact dermatitis

Concept: ELISA quantifies antigens: antigen captured on plate → 1° Ab → enzyme-conjugated 2° Ab → substrate → color change proportional to antigen concentration.
Notes: ELISA: antigen on plate → 1° Ab → 2° Ab-enzyme → substrate → color ∝ antigen conc

Concept: Quorum sensing: bacteria secrete autoinducers that accumulate; threshold conc → population-wide gene expression (biofilm, virulence, sporulation).
Notes: quorum sensing: autoinducers accumulate → threshold conc → coordinated gene expression; biofilm/virulence

Concept: Phagocytosis: FcγR or CR3 receptor ligation → actin remodeling → phagosome formation → lysosome fusion → phagolysosome → hydrolase-mediated killing.
Notes: phagocytosis: FcγR/CR3 → actin remodel → phagosome → lysosome fusion → hydrolase killing

Concept: The complement membrane attack complex (MAC) forms transmembrane pores in pathogen membranes via C5b-9 complex, causing osmotic lysis.
Notes: MAC (C5b-9): transmembrane pores in pathogen membrane → osmotic lysis; CD59 protects host cells

Concept: Flow cytometry: laser excites fluorescent-tagged cells; FSC = cell size; SSC = granularity; fluorescent channels detect surface/intracellular markers; FACS sorts live cells.
Notes: flow cytometry: laser excitation; FSC=size; SSC=granularity; fluorescent channels = markers; FACS = live sort

Concept: The Born-Haber cycle applies Hess's law to calculate lattice energies of ionic compounds from ionization energy, electron affinity, and enthalpy of formation.
Notes: Born-Haber cycle: Hess's law → lattice energy; components: IE + EA + ΔH_f

Concept: Nucleophilic aromatic substitution (SNAr) requires electron-withdrawing groups to activate the ring; proceeds via Meisenheimer complex intermediate.
Notes: SNAr: EWGs activate ring; nucleophile attacks → Meisenheimer complex → leaving group departs

Concept: The Nernst equation: E = E° - (RT/nF)ln(Q); at 25°C simplifies to E = E° - (0.0592/n)log(Q); connects electrode potential to reaction quotient.
Notes: Nernst: E = E° - (0.0592/n)log(Q) at 25°C; cell potential ↔ Q; non-standard conditions

Concept: Enantiomers are non-superimposable mirror images; identical physical properties except optical rotation; may have different pharmacological activity (thalidomide).
Notes: enantiomers: non-superimposable mirrors; same phys. properties except optical rotation; ≠ bio activity (thalidomide)

Concept: Chromatography separates based on differential partitioning between stationary and mobile phases; polarity determines retention time in normal-phase HPLC.
Notes: chromatography: differential partitioning stationary/mobile; retention time ∝ polarity (normal-phase HPLC)

Concept: Oxidative stress: ROS exceed antioxidant capacity → damage to lipids (peroxidation), proteins (carbonylation), DNA (8-oxoguanine).
Notes: oxidative stress: ROS > antioxidants → lipid peroxidation + protein carbonylation + DNA 8-oxoG

Concept: NMR spectroscopy: chemical shift δ reflects electronic environment; coupling constant J shows neighboring proton count; integration = relative proton numbers.
Notes: 1H NMR: δ = electronic environment; J = neighbor H count; integration = relative proton ratio

Concept: Polymer dispersity index PDI = Mw/Mn; PDI = 1 = perfectly monodisperse; living polymerization minimizes PDI; broad PDI = broad MW distribution.
Notes: PDI = Mw/Mn; PDI=1 = monodisperse; living polymerization → low PDI; ↑ PDI = broader distribution

Concept: The Gibbs free energy ΔG = ΔH - TΔS determines spontaneity; ΔG < 0 = spontaneous; ΔG = 0 = equilibrium; ΔG > 0 = non-spontaneous.
Notes: ΔG = ΔH - TΔS; ΔG<0 = spontaneous; ΔG=0 = eq; ΔG>0 = non-spontaneous

Concept: Colligative properties depend only on solute particle concentration, not identity; include boiling point elevation (ΔTb = Kb·m), freezing point depression (ΔTf = Kf·m), osmotic pressure.
Notes: colligative props: ∝ solute particle conc (not identity); ΔTb = Kb·m; ΔTf = Kf·m; π = MRT

Concept: Electrophilic aromatic substitution: electrophile attacks pi system → arenium ion (sigma complex) → deprotonation restores aromaticity.
Notes: EAS: E+ attacks π system → arenium ion (σ complex) → deprotonation → aromaticity restored

Concept: Henderson-Hasselbalch: pH = pKa + log([A-]/[HA]); buffer most effective ±1 pH unit of pKa; max buffer capacity at pH = pKa.
Notes: Henderson-Hasselbalch: pH = pKa + log([A-]/[HA]); best buffer ±1 unit of pKa; max capacity at pH=pKa

Concept: Acid-catalyzed hydration of alkenes follows Markovnikov's rule: H+ adds to less substituted carbon, OH adds to more substituted carbon; proceeds via carbocation.
Notes: acid-catalyzed hydration: H+ → less substituted C; OH → more substituted C; Markovnikov; carbocation intermediate

Concept: Ksp defines solubility equilibrium for slightly soluble salts; ionic product Q > Ksp → precipitation; Q < Ksp → dissolution; Q = Ksp → saturation.
Notes: Ksp: Q > Ksp → precipitate; Q < Ksp → dissolves; Q = Ksp = saturation

Concept: Rate law: rate = k[A]^m[B]^n; order determined experimentally, not from stoichiometry; k increases exponentially with temperature (Arrhenius).
Notes: rate law: rate = k[A]^m[B]^n; orders = experimental (not stoichiometric); k ∝ Arrhenius (↑ temp → ↑ k)

Concept: Carbonyl chemistry: aldehydes more reactive than ketones toward nucleophilic addition; RCHO + Nu- → tetrahedral intermediate; electron-withdrawing groups ↑ electrophilicity.
Notes: carbonyl reactivity: aldehyde > ketone; Nu- → tetrahedral intermediate; EWGs → ↑ electrophilicity

Concept: Peptide bond formation via condensation: amino group attacks carboxyl → tetrahedral intermediate → loss of water → amide bond; reverse = hydrolysis.
Notes: peptide bond: condensation rxn; -NH2 attacks -COOH → tetrahedral intermediate → -H2O → amide bond

Concept: Chirality at sp3 carbon with 4 different substituents; R/S designation by CIP priority rules; racemic mixture = equal R and S; no net optical rotation.
Notes: chirality: sp3 C + 4 different substituents; R/S by CIP rules; racemic mix = equal R+S → no optical rotation

Concept: Le Chatelier's principle: equilibrium shifts to oppose stress; add reactant → shift right; remove product → shift right; ↑ pressure → shift toward fewer gas moles.
Notes: Le Chatelier: stress → equilibrium shifts to oppose; add reactant → right; ↑ P → toward fewer gas moles

Concept: The SN1 mechanism: ionization to carbocation (rate-determining step) → nucleophilic attack; favored by tertiary substrates, polar protic solvents; racemization occurs.
Notes: SN1: ionization → carbocation (RDS) → Nu attack; tertiary + polar protic solvent; racemization

Concept: Oxidation state rules: O = -2; H = +1; sum = overall charge; oxidation = ↑ oxidation state (electron loss); reduction = ↓ oxidation state (electron gain).
Notes: oxidation state: O=-2; H=+1; sum=charge; oxidation=OIL (↑ ox state); reduction=RIG (↓ ox state)

Concept: Grignard reagents (RMgX) are strong nucleophiles and bases; react with CO2 → carboxylic acid; with aldehyde → secondary alcohol; with ketone → tertiary alcohol.
Notes: Grignard (RMgX): strong Nu/base; + CO2 → carboxylic acid; + aldehyde → 2° alcohol; + ketone → 3° alcohol

Concept: Entropy of a system: ΔS > 0 when disorder increases (gas > liquid > solid; mixing; dissolution); second law: universe ΔS never decreases.
Notes: entropy: ΔS > 0 → ↑ disorder; gas > liquid > solid; mixing/dissolution ↑ S; 2nd law: ΔS_universe ≥ 0

Concept: Acidity trend: pKa decreases (stronger acid) with electron-withdrawing groups, resonance stabilization of conjugate base, induction, and high s-character.
Notes: acidity ↑ (pKa ↓) w/: EWGs, resonance-stabilized conjugate base, induction, ↑ s-character

Concept: Titration equivalence point: moles acid = moles base; strong acid + strong base → pH 7; weak acid + strong base → pH > 7 (conjugate base hydrolysis).
Notes: titration equiv point: mol acid = mol base; SA+SB → pH 7; WA+SB → pH>7 (conjugate base hydrolyzes)

Concept: The Bohr model: electrons in fixed energy orbits; photon emitted when electron drops to lower energy level; E = hν; quantized angular momentum.
Notes: Bohr model: electrons in fixed orbits; drop to lower level → photon emitted; E=hν; L = nℏ

Concept: Gibbs free energy and equilibrium: ΔG° = -RT ln(K); K > 1 means ΔG° < 0 (products favored at standard conditions); K relates to cell potential via ΔG° = -nFE°.
Notes: ΔG° = -RT ln K; K>1 → ΔG°<0; ΔG° = -nFE°; links thermodynamics, equilibrium, electrochemistry

Concept: Boiling point elevation and vapor pressure lowering are colligative properties; Raoult's law: P_solution = χ_solvent · P°_solvent; solute ↓ vapor pressure.
Notes: Raoult's law: P_solution = χ_solvent × P°_solvent; ↑ solute → ↓ vapor pressure → ↑ bp, ↓ fp

Concept: Hard-soft acid-base (HSAB) theory: hard acids prefer hard bases; soft acids prefer soft bases; predicts complex stability and reaction outcomes.
Notes: HSAB: hard acid + hard base preferred; soft acid + soft base preferred; predicts stability

Concept: Transition metal complexes: crystal field splitting (Δ) between t2g and eg orbitals; Δ depends on metal oxidation state + ligand field strength (spectrochemical series).
Notes: crystal field: t2g vs eg splitting (Δ); Δ ∝ metal ox state + ligand (spectrochemical series: I- < Br- < F- < OH- < CO < CN-)

Concept: The Diels-Alder reaction: [4+2] cycloaddition between diene (s-cis conformation) and dienophile (electron-poor); stereospecific suprafacial on both components.
Notes: Diels-Alder: [4+2] cycloaddition; diene (s-cis) + electron-poor dienophile; stereospecific; concerted suprafacial

Concept: Hybridization: sp3 = tetrahedral 109.5°; sp2 = trigonal planar 120°; sp = linear 180°; lone pairs occupy sp3 hybrid orbitals in water/ammonia.
Notes: hybridization: sp3=tetrahedral 109.5°; sp2=planar 120°; sp=linear 180°; H2O/NH3 lone pairs in sp3

Concept: Hess's law: enthalpy is a state function; ΔH_rxn = ΣΔH_f(products) - ΣΔH_f(reactants); path independent.
Notes: Hess's law: ΔH = state function; ΔH_rxn = ΣΔH_f(products) - ΣΔH_f(reactants); path independent

Concept: Electrode potentials: higher E° = better oxidizing agent; spontaneous reaction pairs oxidizing agent w/ high E° and reducing agent w/ low E°; E°_cell = E°_cathode - E°_anode.
Notes: electrode: ↑ E° = better oxidizing agent; E°_cell = E°_cathode - E°_anode; spontaneous if E°_cell > 0

Concept: Chemical kinetics: zero-order rate = k; first-order rate = k[A]; second-order rate = k[A]^2; half-life t½ = 0.693/k for first-order reactions.
Notes: kinetics: 0th order: rate=k; 1st order: rate=k[A]; 2nd order: rate=k[A]²; t½(1st order)=0.693/k

Concept: Periodic trends: atomic radius ↓ across period (↑ nuclear charge); ↑ down group (↑ electron shells); ionization energy ↑ across period; EA ↑ across period.
Notes: periodic trends: atomic radius ↓ across period (↑ Z); ↑ down group (shells); IE ↑ across; EA ↑ across

Concept: Protein denaturation disrupts non-covalent interactions (H-bonds, hydrophobic, ionic) and disulfide bonds; destroys tertiary structure; irreversible at high temperature.
Notes: protein denaturation: disrupts H-bonds/hydrophobic/ionic + disulfide bonds; destroys 3° structure; irreversible at ↑ temp

Concept: Conjugated dienes absorb UV light via pi-pi* transitions; longer conjugation = bathochromic shift (red shift); basis of UV-Vis spectroscopy.
Notes: conjugated dienes: π→π* UV absorption; longer conjugation → bathochromic shift (↑ wavelength); UV-Vis basis

Concept: Mass spectrometry: ionization → m/z separation; molecular ion peak [M+] = MW; fragmentation patterns identify functional groups; base peak = most abundant fragment.
Notes: MS: ionization → m/z; M+ = MW; fragmentation → functional group ID; base peak = most abundant fragment

Concept: Polymers: addition polymerization (vinyl monomers, no byproduct); condensation polymerization (bifunctional monomers, releases small molecule like H2O or HCl).
Notes: addition polymerization: vinyl monomers, no byproduct; condensation: bifunctional monomers + small molecule byproduct

Concept: Water properties explained by hydrogen bonding: high bp (100°C), high surface tension, high heat capacity (4.18 J/g°C), density anomaly (ice < liquid water).
Notes: water: H-bonds → ↑ bp (100°C), ↑ surface tension, ↑ heat capacity (4.18 J/g°C), ice less dense than liquid

Concept: Electronegativity drives bond polarity; bond dipoles sum to molecular dipole moment; symmetric molecules (BF3, CCl4) = nonpolar despite polar bonds.
Notes: electronegativity → bond polarity; dipoles sum to molecular dipole; symmetric molecules (BF3, CCl4) = nonpolar

Concept: The Henderson-Hasselbalch equation in biochemistry: intracellular pH ~7.2; blood pH 7.35-7.45 maintained by bicarbonate buffer (H2CO3/HCO3-).
Notes: blood buffer: bicarbonate system (H2CO3/HCO3-); pH 7.35-7.45; Henderson-Hasselbalch; CO2 controls pH

Concept: Oxidative phosphorylation: NADH → CI (~2.5 ATP); FADH2 → CII (~1.5 ATP); H+ gradient across inner mitochondrial membrane drives ATP synthase (F0F1).
Notes: oxphos: NADH→CI→~2.5 ATP; FADH2→CII→~1.5 ATP; H+ gradient → ATP synthase (F0F1); inner membrane

Concept: Friedel-Crafts alkylation: R-X + Lewis acid (AlCl3) → carbocation electrophile → EAS on benzene; subject to polyalkylation and carbocation rearrangement.
Notes: Friedel-Crafts alkylation: RX + AlCl3 → R+ → EAS on benzene; issues: polyalkylation + carbocation rearrangement

Concept: Michaelis-Menten: v = Vmax[S]/(Km + [S]); Km = [S] at ½Vmax; low Km = high affinity; Vmax = maximum velocity; Lineweaver-Burk linearizes for inhibitor studies.
Notes: Michaelis-Menten: v = Vmax[S]/(Km+[S]); Km=[S] at ½Vmax; ↓Km=↑ affinity; Lineweaver-Burk linearizes

Concept: Infrared spectroscopy identifies functional groups via bond vibration frequencies; O-H stretch ~3300 cm-1; C=O ~1700 cm-1; C-H ~2900 cm-1.
Notes: IR: functional group ID via bond vibration; O-H ~3300 cm-1; C=O ~1700 cm-1; C-H ~2900 cm-1

Concept: Supercritical fluids have properties between liquid and gas; CO2 at Tc=31.1°C, Pc=73.8 bar; used in green chemistry extraction, chromatography.
Notes: supercritical fluids: between liquid + gas; CO2: Tc=31.1°C, Pc=73.8 bar; green extraction + SFC

Concept: Coordination chemistry: coordination number = number of ligand donor atoms; octahedral = CN6; tetrahedral = CN4; square planar = CN4 (d8 metals).
Notes: coordination chem: CN = ligand donor atoms; octahedral=CN6; tetrahedral=CN4; square planar=CN4 (d8 metals)

Concept: Radioactive decay: alpha decay (helium-4 nucleus emission, ↓ A by 4, Z by 2); beta decay (neutron → proton + electron + antineutrino); gamma (photon emission).
Notes: radioactive decay: α = He-4 emission (↓A 4, ↓Z 2); β = n → p + e- + antineutrino; γ = photon emission

Concept: Phase diagrams: triple point = solid/liquid/gas coexist; critical point = liquid/gas distinction disappears; slope of solid-liquid boundary indicates relative densities.
Notes: phase diagram: triple point = S/L/G coexist; critical point = L/G indistinguishable; S-L slope indicates density

Concept: The Lorentz force on a charged particle: F = q(E + v×B); electric component qE acts along field; magnetic component qv×B is perpendicular to both velocity and field.
Notes: Lorentz force: F = q(E + v×B); electric = qE along field; magnetic = qv×B ⊥ to v and B

Concept: Faraday's law of induction: EMF = -dΦ_B/dt; changing magnetic flux induces EMF; Lenz's law states induced current opposes flux change.
Notes: Faraday: EMF = -dΦ_B/dt; ↑ flux → induced EMF opposes change (Lenz's law)

Concept: The virial theorem: for stable gravitationally bound systems ⟨KE⟩ = -½⟨PE⟩; total energy = ½⟨PE⟩; applies to galaxies, gas clouds, plasma.
Notes: virial theorem: ⟨KE⟩ = -½⟨PE⟩; total E = ½⟨PE⟩; applies to gravitationally bound systems

Concept: Nuclear binding energy per nucleon peaks at Fe-56; lighter nuclei release energy via fusion; heavier nuclei release energy via fission.
Notes: nuclear binding: peaks at Fe-56; lighter nuclei → energy via fusion; heavier nuclei → energy via fission

Concept: Quantum tunneling: particle probability amplitude decays exponentially through classically forbidden barrier; enables nuclear fusion at stellar temperatures, STM, alpha decay.
Notes: quantum tunneling: amplitude decays through barrier; enables stellar fusion, STM, alpha decay

Concept: The Compton effect: X-ray scatters off electron → wavelength increases; Δλ = (h/m_e·c)(1-cosθ); confirms photon has momentum.
Notes: Compton: X-ray + e- → ↑ wavelength; Δλ = (h/m_e·c)(1-cosθ); proves photon momentum

Concept: Kirchhoff's laws: KVL (sum of voltage drops around loop = 0); KCL (sum of currents into node = 0); conservation of energy and charge respectively.
Notes: Kirchhoff: KVL = Σ voltages in loop = 0 (energy conservation); KCL = Σ currents at node = 0 (charge conservation)

Concept: In special relativity, length contraction: L = L₀/γ; moving object appears shorter along direction of motion; perpendicular dimensions unchanged.
Notes: length contraction: L = L₀/γ; moving object shorter along motion direction; transverse dims unchanged

Concept: The de Broglie wavelength: λ = h/p = h/mv; matter has wave properties; electron diffraction confirms this; heavier or faster objects → shorter wavelength.
Notes: de Broglie: λ = h/p = h/mv; matter has wave properties; ↑ mass or ↑ v → ↓ λ; confirmed by electron diffraction

Concept: Snell's law: n₁sinθ₁ = n₂sinθ₂; total internal reflection when θ > θ_c = arcsin(n₂/n₁); applied in fiber optics and diamond cutting.
Notes: Snell's law: n₁sinθ₁ = n₂sinθ₂; TIR when θ > arcsin(n₂/n₁); fiber optics, diamond

Concept: The photoelectric effect: photon energy must exceed work function Φ to eject electron; KE_max = hf - Φ; threshold frequency f₀ = Φ/h.
Notes: photoelectric: hf > Φ → eject e-; KE_max = hf - Φ; threshold f₀ = Φ/h; proves photon particle nature

Concept: Wien's displacement law: λ_max = b/T (b = 2.898×10⁻³ m·K); hotter blackbody → peak wavelength shifts to shorter wavelengths (blue shift).
Notes: Wien's law: λ_max = b/T; ↑ T → ↓ λ_max (blue shift); b = 2.898×10⁻³ m·K

Concept: The Pauli exclusion principle: no two identical fermions can occupy the same quantum state; basis of periodic table structure and white dwarf stability.
Notes: Pauli exclusion: no two identical fermions in same quantum state; → periodic table + white dwarf stability

Concept: Einstein's photoelectric equation links quantum energy to frequency; E = hf; Planck's constant h = 6.626×10⁻³⁴ J·s; energy quantized in photons.
Notes: E = hf; Planck's h = 6.626×10⁻³⁴ J·s; energy quantized in photons; explains photoelectric threshold

Concept: The Bernoulli equation: P + ½ρv² + ρgh = constant along streamline; valid for incompressible, inviscid, steady flow; higher velocity → lower pressure.
Notes: Bernoulli: P + ½ρv² + ρgh = const; ↑ v → ↓ P; incompressible, inviscid, steady flow

Concept: Black hole event horizon: Schwarzschild radius r_s = 2GM/c²; nothing escapes inside including light; Hawking radiation (thermal) emitted near horizon via quantum effects.
Notes: black hole: Schwarzschild r = 2GM/c²; nothing escapes interior; Hawking radiation = quantum thermal emission near horizon

Concept: The uncertainty principle applies to energy and time: ΔE·Δt ≥ ħ/2; virtual particles can exist briefly; basis of quantum vacuum fluctuations and Casimir effect.
Notes: energy-time uncertainty: ΔE·Δt ≥ ħ/2; virtual particles exist briefly; → vacuum fluctuations + Casimir effect

Concept: Ohm's law: V = IR; resistance R = ρL/A (ρ = resistivity, L = length, A = cross-section); conductance G = 1/R; power P = IV = I²R = V²/R.
Notes: Ohm's law: V=IR; R=ρL/A; G=1/R; power P=IV=I²R=V²/R

Concept: The Doppler shift formula: observed frequency f = f₀(v±v_observer)/(v∓v_source); redshift = recession; blueshift = approach; used in cosmology and radar.
Notes: Doppler: f = f₀(v±v_obs)/(v∓v_src); recession→redshift; approach→blueshift; cosmology + radar

Concept: Diffraction grating equation: dsinθ = mλ; bright fringes at integer multiples; resolving power R = mN (m = order, N = lines); separates wavelengths.
Notes: diffraction grating: dsinθ = mλ; fringes at integer m; resolving power R=mN; separates wavelengths

Concept: The Stefan-Boltzmann law: radiated power P = σεAT⁴; σ = 5.67×10⁻⁸ W/m²K⁴; ε = emissivity (1 for blackbody); P ∝ T⁴.
Notes: Stefan-Boltzmann: P = σεAT⁴; σ=5.67×10⁻⁸ W/m²K⁴; P ∝ T⁴; blackbody ε=1

Concept: Young's double-slit experiment demonstrates light interference; bright fringes at y = mλL/d; confirms wave nature of light; also works for electrons.
Notes: double-slit: bright fringes at y=mλL/d; constructive interference; confirms wave nature of light + electrons

Concept: First law of thermodynamics: ΔU = Q - W; internal energy change = heat absorbed minus work done by system; conservation of energy for thermodynamic systems.
Notes: 1st law: ΔU = Q - W; energy conserved; heat in → ↑ internal energy; work out → ↓ internal energy

Concept: Magnetic flux density B; force on current-carrying wire F = BIL sinθ; force between parallel wires; basis of electric motor and galvanometer operation.
Notes: magnetic force: F = BIL sinθ on current wire; parallel wires attract if same direction; motor + galvanometer basis

Concept: Electrostatic force: Coulomb's law F = kq₁q₂/r² (k = 8.99×10⁹ N·m²/C²); inverse square law; superposition principle applies.
Notes: Coulomb: F = kq₁q₂/r²; k=8.99×10⁹; inverse square law; superposition principle

Concept: The ideal gas law PV = nRT links pressure, volume, moles, and temperature; R = 8.314 J/mol·K; derived from kinetic theory assuming elastic collisions and no intermolecular forces.
Notes: ideal gas: PV=nRT; R=8.314 J/mol·K; assumes elastic collisions + no intermolecular forces

Concept: Maxwell's equations in differential form: Gauss (∇·E = ρ/ε₀); no monopoles (∇·B = 0); Faraday (∇×E = -∂B/∂t); Ampère-Maxwell (∇×B = μ₀J + μ₀ε₀∂E/∂t).
Notes: Maxwell eqs: ∇·E=ρ/ε₀ (Gauss); ∇·B=0 (no monopoles); ∇×E=-∂B/∂t (Faraday); ∇×B=μ₀J+μ₀ε₀∂E/∂t (Ampère-Maxwell)

Concept: The principle of superposition: total wave displacement = algebraic sum of individual waves; constructive interference (same phase); destructive (opposite phase).
Notes: superposition: total displacement = sum of individual waves; constructive = same phase; destructive = opposite phase

Concept: Gravitational potential energy: U = -GMm/r; negative because bound systems; escape velocity v = √(2GM/r); for Earth ~11.2 km/s.
Notes: gravitational PE: U = -GMm/r (negative, bound); escape velocity v=√(2GM/r); Earth ~11.2 km/s

Concept: The third law of thermodynamics: entropy of a perfect crystal at absolute zero = 0; impossible to reach T = 0 K in a finite number of steps.
Notes: 3rd law: perfect crystal at 0 K → S=0; impossible to reach absolute zero in finite steps

Concept: Centripetal acceleration: a = v²/r = ω²r directed inward; centripetal force F = mv²/r provided by tension, gravity, or normal force.
Notes: centripetal: a = v²/r = ω²r (inward); F = mv²/r; provided by tension/gravity/normal force

Concept: Resonance in LC circuits: f₀ = 1/(2π√LC); at resonance impedance is minimum (series) or maximum (parallel); quality factor Q = ω₀L/R.
Notes: LC resonance: f₀ = 1/(2π√LC); series resonance: Z minimum; parallel: Z maximum; Q = ω₀L/R
