Concept: Rutherford scattering experiment: alpha particles scattered by thin gold foil; large-angle scattering proved existence of dense positive nucleus; disproved Thomson model.
Notes: Rutherford: alpha scattering off gold foil; large-angle scatter → dense positive nucleus; disproved Thomson 'plum pudding'

Concept: Capacitance: C = Q/V = ε₀A/d for parallel plates; stored energy U = ½CV² = Q²/2C; dielectric increases capacitance by factor κ.
Notes: capacitance: C=Q/V=ε₀A/d; energy U=½CV²=Q²/2C; dielectric → C×κ

Concept: Simple harmonic motion: F = -kx; period T = 2π√(m/k) for spring; T = 2π√(L/g) for pendulum; energy oscillates between KE and PE.
Notes: SHM: F=-kx; spring T=2π√(m/k); pendulum T=2π√(L/g); energy ↔ KE and PE

Concept: Polarization of light: transverse waves can be polarized; Malus's law I = I₀cos²θ; Brewster's angle tan(θ_B) = n₂/n₁ gives complete polarization on reflection.
Notes: polarization: transverse waves; Malus: I=I₀cos²θ; Brewster's angle tanθ_B=n₂/n₁ → fully polarized reflection

Concept: Wave-particle duality: light shows diffraction/interference (wave) and photoelectric effect (particle); electrons show diffraction (wave) and collision behavior (particle).
Notes: wave-particle duality: light = waves (diffraction) + particles (photoelectric); electrons = waves (diffraction) + particles (collision)

Concept: The second law of thermodynamics (Clausius): heat flows spontaneously from hot to cold; entropy of isolated system never decreases; heat engines < 100% efficient.
Notes: 2nd law (Clausius): heat hot→cold spontaneous; ΔS_isolated ≥ 0; heat engines < 100% efficient

Concept: Impulse-momentum theorem: J = FΔt = Δp; impulse equals change in momentum; airbags and crumple zones increase collision time → reduce peak force.
Notes: impulse-momentum: J = FΔt = Δp; ↑ collision time → ↓ peak force; airbags/crumple zones

Concept: Angular momentum L = Iω = mvr; conserved when no net torque; ice skater spins faster when arms pulled in (↓I → ↑ω); precession in gyroscopes.
Notes: angular momentum: L=Iω=mvr; conserved w/ no net torque; ↓I → ↑ω (skater); gyroscope precession

Concept: Radioactive decay law: N(t) = N₀e^(-λt); half-life t½ = ln2/λ = 0.693/λ; activity A = λN; used in radiometric dating.
Notes: radioactive decay: N(t)=N₀e^(-λt); t½=0.693/λ; activity A=λN; radiometric dating application

Concept: The Josephson effect: superconducting junction conducts DC current without voltage (DC Josephson); AC current when voltage applied (AC Josephson); basis of SQUIDs.
Notes: Josephson effect: DC = no-voltage current across superconducting junction; AC = current w/ voltage; basis of SQUIDs

Concept: Magnification in optics: m = -v/u = f/(f-u); convex lens (positive f) creates real/inverted image for u > f; virtual/upright for u < f.
Notes: lens magnification: m = -v/u; convex lens (f>0): real+inverted if u>f; virtual+upright if u<f

Concept: The continuity equation for fluids: A₁v₁ = A₂v₂; conservation of mass for incompressible fluid; narrow pipe → faster flow.
Notes: continuity: A₁v₁ = A₂v₂; mass conservation; ↓ area → ↑ velocity in incompressible fluid

Concept: Standing waves on string: λ_n = 2L/n; f_n = nv/2L; nodes at fixed points; antinodes at maximum displacement; fundamental = n=1.
Notes: standing waves: λ_n = 2L/n; f_n = nv/2L; nodes at fixed ends; fundamental n=1

Concept: The Meissner effect: superconductors expel magnetic fields (B=0 inside) below T_c; perfect diamagnetism; distinguishes superconductors from perfect conductors.
Notes: Meissner effect: B=0 inside superconductor below T_c; perfect diamagnetism; ≠ perfect conductor

Concept: Special relativity mass-energy: E = mc²; rest energy of 1 kg = 9×10¹⁶ J; mass-energy equivalence; total energy E = γmc²; kinetic energy KE = (γ-1)mc².
Notes: E=mc²; rest energy 1 kg = 9×10¹⁶ J; total E=γmc²; KE=(γ-1)mc²

Concept: The Hall effect: magnetic field on current-carrying conductor → transverse voltage; measures charge carrier density n = IB/qV_H·t; identifies carrier type.
Notes: Hall effect: B ⊥ I → transverse voltage V_H; n=IB/qV_H·t; identifies carrier type (e- vs holes)

Concept: Transistor operation (NPN BJT): base current controls large collector-emitter current; current gain β = I_C/I_B; used as switch or amplifier.
Notes: NPN BJT: I_B controls I_C; β = I_C/I_B (current gain); switch or amplifier

Concept: Gravitational waves: ripples in spacetime caused by accelerating masses; detected by LIGO (interferometry); first detected 2015 from binary black hole merger.
Notes: gravitational waves: spacetime ripples from accelerating masses; LIGO (interferometry); first detected 2015 (BH merger)

Concept: The P vs NP problem asks whether every problem whose solution can be verified in polynomial time can also be solved in polynomial time; unsolved since 1971.
Notes: P vs NP: P = poly-time solvable; NP = poly-time verifiable; P=NP? Millennium Prize; unsolved since 1971

Concept: Dijkstra's algorithm: greedy shortest path on non-negative weighted graphs; uses priority queue; time O((V+E) log V); fails with negative edges.
Notes: Dijkstra: greedy shortest path; non-negative edges; priority queue; O((V+E) log V); fails w/ negative edges

Concept: The Byzantine generals problem: distributed consensus in presence of traitors; n nodes tolerate f faulty nodes if n ≥ 3f+1; basis of BFT protocols.
Notes: Byzantine fault: n nodes tolerate f traitors if n≥3f+1; consensus despite faulty nodes; basis of BFT

Concept: Consistent hashing: nodes and keys on same ring; key → first clockwise node; adding/removing a node only remaps 1/n keys; used in distributed caches.
Notes: consistent hashing: ring; key → first clockwise node; add/remove → remaps only 1/n keys; distributed caches

Concept: The CAP theorem: distributed systems choose 2 of 3: Consistency (all nodes same data), Availability (always responds), Partition Tolerance (survives network splits).
Notes: CAP: pick 2 of 3: Consistency (same data) + Availability (always responds) + Partition Tolerance; can't have all

Concept: ACID: Atomicity (all or nothing), Consistency (valid state preserved), Isolation (concurrent transactions don't interfere), Durability (committed data survives crash).
Notes: ACID: Atomicity (all-or-nothing) + Consistency (valid) + Isolation (no interference) + Durability (crash survives)

Concept: Bloom filter: bit array + k hash functions; test membership in O(k); false positives possible, false negatives impossible; space-efficient probabilistic structure.
Notes: Bloom filter: bit array + k hashes; O(k) membership; false positives possible, false negatives never; space-efficient

Concept: The two-phase commit (2PC): prepare phase (all nodes vote) → commit phase (if all yes) or abort phase (if any no); blocks on coordinator failure.
Notes: 2PC: prepare (all vote) → commit if all yes else abort; blocking on coordinator failure; distributed atomicity

Concept: The OSI 7-layer model: Physical → Data Link → Network → Transport → Session → Presentation → Application; encapsulation adds header each layer down.
Notes: OSI 7 layers: Physical→Data Link→Network (IP)→Transport (TCP)→Session→Presentation→Application; encapsulation ↓

Concept: SQL joins: INNER = only matching rows; LEFT = all left + matching right; RIGHT = all right + matching left; FULL OUTER = all rows from both tables.
Notes: SQL joins: INNER=matching only; LEFT=all left+match right; RIGHT=all right+match left; FULL OUTER=all rows

Concept: B-tree: balanced tree; branching factor t; all operations O(log_t n); sorted data; external memory efficient; used in database indexing (InnoDB, PostgreSQL).
Notes: B-tree: balanced; branching t; O(log_t n) all ops; sorted; disk-efficient; DB indexing (InnoDB/PostgreSQL)

Concept: Map-Reduce: Map(key, value) → list(intermediate key, value); Reduce(key, list(values)) → output; parallelizable; fault-tolerant; Google origin.
Notes: MapReduce: Map → (intermediate k,v) pairs; Reduce → aggregates by key; parallel + fault-tolerant; Google

Concept: Docker: packages app + dependencies into container sharing host OS kernel; image = blueprint; container = running instance; lighter + faster than VMs.
Notes: Docker: app + deps in container; shares host kernel; image=blueprint; container=running instance; lighter than VM

Concept: Kubernetes orchestrates containers: scheduling (place on nodes), scaling (auto), self-healing (restart failed), service discovery, load balancing; declarative config.
Notes: Kubernetes: container orchestration; scheduling + scaling + self-healing + service discovery; declarative YAML config

Concept: TCP three-way handshake: SYN → SYN-ACK → ACK establishes connection; FIN-ACK-FIN-ACK for teardown; reliable ordered delivery with congestion control.
Notes: TCP: 3-way handshake SYN→SYN-ACK→ACK; FIN×2 teardown; reliable ordered delivery + congestion control

Concept: Public key infrastructure: RSA uses public key to encrypt, private key to decrypt; security = hard factoring n=p×q; key exchange via Diffie-Hellman.
Notes: RSA: public encrypt, private decrypt; security = factoring n=p×q; key exchange → Diffie-Hellman

Concept: Garbage collection: reference counting (fast, no cycle detection); mark-and-sweep (handles cycles, stop-the-world pause); generational GC (young/old generations).
Notes: GC: ref counting (fast, no cycle detect); mark-and-sweep (cycles ok, pause); generational (young→old); trade-offs

Concept: The Raft consensus algorithm: leader election → log replication → commitment; simpler than Paxos; leader handles all client requests; used in etcd, CockroachDB.
Notes: Raft: leader election → log replication → commit; simpler than Paxos; used in etcd/CockroachDB

Concept: Mutex (mutual exclusion lock): only one thread holds it; binary semaphore owned by locking thread; prevents race conditions; risk = deadlock if not released.
Notes: mutex: binary, owner-thread only; prevents race conditions; risk = deadlock; ≠ semaphore (any thread can signal)

Concept: REST API constraints: stateless (no session state), client-server, cacheable, uniform interface (resources identified by URIs), layered, optional code-on-demand.
Notes: REST: stateless + client-server + cacheable + uniform interface (URI resources) + layered; optional: code-on-demand

Concept: Cache eviction policies: LRU (least recently used) → evict oldest accessed; LFU (least frequently used) → evict least accessed; FIFO → evict oldest inserted.
Notes: cache eviction: LRU = evict oldest accessed; LFU = evict least accessed; FIFO = evict oldest inserted

Concept: The merge sort algorithm: divide array in half recursively → merge sorted halves; stable sort; O(n log n) all cases; O(n) extra space; parallelizable.
Notes: merge sort: divide → merge; stable; O(n log n) always; O(n) space; parallelizable

Concept: Graph algorithms: BFS = shortest path (unweighted), level-order; DFS = cycle detection, topological sort; both O(V+E); BFS uses queue, DFS uses stack/recursion.
Notes: BFS: shortest path (unweighted), queue, O(V+E); DFS: cycle detect + toposort, stack/recursion, O(V+E)

Concept: The AES encryption: symmetric block cipher; 128/192/256-bit keys; 10/12/14 rounds of SubBytes, ShiftRows, MixColumns, AddRoundKey; current standard.
Notes: AES: symmetric block cipher; 128/192/256-bit keys; 10/12/14 rounds; SubBytes+ShiftRows+MixColumns+AddRoundKey

Concept: Dynamic programming key properties: optimal substructure (optimal solution built from optimal sub-solutions) + overlapping subproblems (reuse cached results).
Notes: DP: optimal substructure (optimal built from optimal sub-solutions) + overlapping subproblems (memoize); bottom-up or top-down

Concept: Virtual memory: process sees contiguous virtual address space; OS + MMU map to physical pages; page fault triggers swap from disk; TLB caches recent mappings.
Notes: virtual memory: virtual→physical pages via MMU; page fault → swap from disk; TLB caches recent translations

Concept: SQL window functions: ROW_NUMBER(), RANK(), DENSE_RANK(), LAG(), LEAD(), SUM() OVER(PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY ...); compute over a set without GROUP BY collapse.
Notes: SQL window functions: ROW_NUMBER/RANK/DENSE_RANK/LAG/LEAD/SUM OVER(PARTITION BY, ORDER BY); no GROUP BY collapse

Concept: Deadlock conditions (Coffman 1971): mutual exclusion + hold-and-wait + no preemption + circular wait; all 4 must hold; prevention breaks one condition.
Notes: deadlock (Coffman): mutual exclusion + hold-and-wait + no preemption + circular wait; all 4 required; break any → prevent

Concept: The time complexity of quicksort: O(n log n) average, O(n²) worst case (sorted array + naive pivot); randomized pivot or median-of-three avoids worst case.
Notes: quicksort: O(n log n) avg; O(n²) worst (sorted + naive pivot); randomized/median-of-3 pivot prevents worst

Concept: Containerization vs virtualization: VMs virtualize hardware (full OS per VM, GB overhead); containers share host OS kernel (MB overhead, faster startup).
Notes: VMs: virtualize hardware, full OS per VM, GB overhead; containers: share host kernel, MB overhead, faster startup

Concept: The SHA-256 hash function: 256-bit output; deterministic; preimage resistance; collision resistance; avalanche effect; used in Bitcoin mining and TLS.
Notes: SHA-256: 256-bit output; deterministic; preimage + collision resistant; avalanche effect; Bitcoin + TLS

Concept: Regex: ^ anchors start; $ anchors end; .* matches any characters; [abc] character class; | alternation; + one or more; ? optional; () capture group.
Notes: regex: ^=start; $=end; .*=any chars; [abc]=char class; |=alternation; +=1+; ?=optional; ()=capture group

Concept: Microservices: decompose monolith into independent services communicating via APIs; each service owns its data; enables independent deployment and scaling; adds distributed system complexity.
Notes: microservices: independent services via APIs; own data per service; independent deploy/scale; adds distributed complexity

Concept: SOLID principles: Single responsibility; Open/closed (open for extension, closed for modification); Liskov substitution; Interface segregation; Dependency inversion.
Notes: SOLID: Single responsibility + Open/closed + Liskov substitution + Interface segregation + Dependency inversion

Concept: The N+1 query problem: loading N records then making N separate queries for each related record; solved by eager loading (JOIN) or batching queries.
Notes: N+1 query: load N records → N extra queries for relations; fix: eager loading (JOIN) or batch queries

Concept: Event-driven architecture: producers emit events to broker (Kafka, RabbitMQ); consumers subscribe and process asynchronously; decouples services; enables replay.
Notes: event-driven: producers → broker (Kafka/RabbitMQ) → consumers; async; decoupled services; enables event replay

Concept: The actor model: each actor has mailbox, processes messages sequentially; no shared state; communicates only via messages; fault tolerant; e.g., Erlang, Akka.
Notes: actor model: mailbox + sequential message processing; no shared state; message-only comms; Erlang/Akka

Concept: Rate limiting strategies: token bucket (burst allowed up to bucket size); leaky bucket (constant outflow rate); sliding window log; fixed window counter.
Notes: rate limiting: token bucket (burst to bucket size); leaky bucket (constant rate); sliding window; fixed window

Concept: The CAP theorem in practice: CP systems (Zookeeper, HBase) sacrifice availability; AP systems (Cassandra, DynamoDB) sacrifice consistency; CA = no partition tolerance (unrealistic).
Notes: CAP practice: CP=Zookeeper/HBase (sacrifice availability); AP=Cassandra/DynamoDB (sacrifice consistency); CA = unrealistic

Concept: Index types in databases: B-tree index (range queries, equality, ORDER BY); hash index (equality only); GiST (geometric, full-text); covering index includes all query columns.
Notes: DB indexes: B-tree (range+equality+sort); hash (equality only); GiST (geometric/text); covering index = all query cols

Concept: WebSocket protocol: full-duplex bidirectional communication over single TCP connection; starts with HTTP upgrade handshake; low overhead vs polling; use for real-time apps.
Notes: WebSocket: full-duplex over TCP; HTTP upgrade handshake; low overhead vs polling; real-time apps

Concept: Load balancing algorithms: round robin (equal distribution); least connections (route to least busy); IP hash (sticky sessions); weighted round robin (capacity-aware).
Notes: load balancing: round robin (equal); least connections (least busy); IP hash (sticky); weighted round robin (capacity)

Concept: Circuit breaker pattern: closed (normal) → open (failing, reject requests) → half-open (test recovery); prevents cascade failures in microservices.
Notes: circuit breaker: closed→open (fail fast)→half-open (test); prevents cascade failures in microservices

Concept: Recursion: function calls itself; base case stops recursion; call stack depth = O(n) memory; tail recursion optimized by compiler to O(1) stack; memoize for DP.
Notes: recursion: self-call + base case; stack depth O(n); tail recursion → compiler optimizes to O(1); memoize for DP

Concept: The CAP theorem holds only for single-partition scenarios; in practice BASE (Basically Available, Soft state, Eventually consistent) describes NoSQL systems.
Notes: BASE: Basically Available + Soft state + Eventually consistent; describes NoSQL; vs ACID for SQL

Concept: Hashing collision resolution: chaining (linked list per bucket, O(n) worst); open addressing: linear probing (+1), quadratic probing (+k²), double hashing.
Notes: hash collision: chaining (linked list, O(n) worst); open addressing: linear probing / quadratic / double hashing

Concept: The Ethernet protocol: CSMA/CD for collision detection on shared medium; switches use MAC address table; VLAN separates broadcast domains; full-duplex eliminates collisions.
Notes: Ethernet: CSMA/CD (collision detect); switches use MAC table; VLAN = separate broadcast domains; full-duplex = no collisions

Concept: Functional programming: pure functions (no side effects), immutability, higher-order functions (map, filter, reduce), referential transparency; easier to reason and test.
Notes: functional programming: pure functions (no side effects) + immutability + HOF (map/filter/reduce) + referential transparency

Concept: Dependency injection: components receive dependencies from outside (constructor, setter, interface injection); inversion of control; improves testability and modularity.
Notes: dependency injection: receive deps externally; IoC; types: constructor/setter/interface; ↑ testability + modularity

Concept: TLS handshake: client hello (cipher suites) → server hello (chosen cipher + certificate) → key exchange (asymmetric) → session keys established → symmetric encryption.
Notes: TLS: client hello → server hello + cert → key exchange (asymmetric) → session keys → symmetric encrypt

Concept: The UNIX philosophy: write programs that do one thing well; write programs to work together; write programs to handle text streams; small composable tools.
Notes: UNIX philosophy: 1 thing well + composable + text streams; small tools chained via pipe

Concept: The Riemann hypothesis conjectures that all non-trivial zeros of the zeta function lie on the critical line Re(s) = 1/2; unsolved; has Millennium Prize.
Notes: Riemann hypothesis: all non-trivial zeros of ζ(s) on Re(s)=1/2; unsolved; Millennium Prize

Concept: Stokes' theorem: ∮_∂Ω ω = ∫_Ω dω; generalizes Green's, divergence, and classical Stokes theorems; relates boundary integral to interior derivative.
Notes: Stokes' theorem: ∮_∂Ω ω = ∫_Ω dω; generalizes Green's/divergence/classical Stokes; boundary ↔ interior

Concept: Complex numbers: z = a + bi; modulus |z| = √(a²+b²); argument θ = arctan(b/a); polar form z = r·e^(iθ) = r(cosθ + i sinθ).
Notes: complex numbers: z=a+bi; |z|=√(a²+b²); θ=arctan(b/a); polar: z=re^(iθ)=r(cosθ+i sinθ)

Concept: The Cauchy-Schwarz inequality: |⟨u,v⟩|² ≤ ⟨u,u⟩·⟨v,v⟩; in R^n: (Σaᵢbᵢ)² ≤ (Σaᵢ²)(Σbᵢ²); equality iff vectors parallel.
Notes: Cauchy-Schwarz: |⟨u,v⟩|² ≤ |u|²|v|²; equality iff u∥v; in R^n: (Σaᵢbᵢ)² ≤ (Σaᵢ²)(Σbᵢ²)

Concept: The divergence theorem: ∯_∂V F·dA = ∭_V (∇·F) dV; relates outward flux through closed surface to divergence within enclosed volume.
Notes: divergence theorem: ∯_∂V F·dA = ∭_V ∇·F dV; surface flux ↔ volume divergence

Concept: Taylor series expansion: f(x) = Σ f^(n)(a)/n! · (x-a)^n; for e^x = Σ x^n/n!; for sin x = x - x³/3! + x⁵/5! - ...; converges in radius of convergence.
Notes: Taylor series: f(x) = Σf^(n)(a)/n!·(x-a)^n; e^x=Σx^n/n!; sinx=x-x³/3!+x⁵/5!-...; valid within radius of convergence

Concept: Matrix multiplication: (AB)ᵢⱼ = Σₖ Aᵢₖ Bₖⱼ; not commutative (AB ≠ BA generally); associative; identity matrix I satisfies AI = IA = A.
Notes: matrix mult: (AB)ᵢⱼ = Σₖ Aᵢₖ Bₖⱼ; not commutative (AB≠BA); associative; AI=IA=A

Concept: The Fundamental Theorem of Algebra: every non-constant polynomial with complex coefficients has at least one complex root; degree n polynomial has exactly n roots (counting multiplicity).
Notes: FT Algebra: every degree-n complex polynomial has n roots (w/ multiplicity); at least 1 complex root always

Concept: Markov chains: system of states with transition probabilities; stationary distribution π satisfies πP = π; irreducible + aperiodic chains → unique stationary distribution.
Notes: Markov chains: states + transition probs P; stationary dist: πP=π; irreducible+aperiodic → unique stationary dist

Concept: Convex optimization: objective function and feasible set both convex; local minimum = global minimum; gradient descent converges; Lagrangian for constrained problems.
Notes: convex optimization: convex f + convex set; local min = global min; gradient descent converges; Lagrangian w/ constraints

Concept: The law of large numbers: sample mean converges to population mean as n → ∞; weak LLN (convergence in probability); strong LLN (almost sure convergence).
Notes: LLN: sample mean → population mean as n→∞; weak LLN (in probability); strong LLN (almost sure)

Concept: Linear algebra rank: rank = number of linearly independent rows = columns; rank-nullity theorem: rank + nullity = n (number of columns); full rank ↔ invertible for square matrices.
Notes: rank: linearly independent rows/cols; rank-nullity: rank + nullity = n; full rank ↔ invertible (square)

Concept: Differential equations: linear ODE y' + p(x)y = q(x); solution via integrating factor μ=e^(∫p dx); second order ay'' + by' + cy = 0; characteristic equation.
Notes: 1st order linear ODE: y' + p(x)y = q(x); integrating factor μ=e^∫p dx; 2nd order: characteristic equation ar²+br+c=0

Concept: The pigeonhole principle: if n items into m containers and n > m, at least one container holds ≥ 2 items; generalized: at least ⌈n/m⌉ items in some container.
Notes: pigeonhole: n items, m containers, n>m → ≥1 container w/ ≥2 items; generalized: ⌈n/m⌉ minimum

Concept: Stirling's approximation: n! ≈ √(2πn)(n/e)^n; useful for combinatorics; ln(n!) ≈ n ln n - n + ½ ln(2πn).
Notes: Stirling: n! ≈ √(2πn)(n/e)^n; ln(n!) ≈ n ln n - n + ½ln(2πn); useful for large factorial estimates

Concept: The Chinese Remainder Theorem: system of congruences x ≡ aᵢ (mod nᵢ) with pairwise coprime moduli has unique solution modulo ∏nᵢ.
Notes: CRT: x≡aᵢ(mod nᵢ) w/ coprime moduli → unique solution mod ∏nᵢ

Concept: Graph theory: Euler path visits every edge once (if 0 or 2 odd-degree vertices); Euler circuit visits every edge once and returns (all even-degree vertices).
Notes: Euler: path visits all edges once = 0 or 2 odd-degree vertices; circuit = all even-degree vertices

Concept: The Gram-Schmidt process: converts linearly independent vectors into orthonormal basis; project and subtract to get orthogonal; normalize each vector.
Notes: Gram-Schmidt: independent vectors → orthonormal basis; subtract projections → orthogonal; then normalize

Concept: Group theory: group (G, ·) requires closure, associativity, identity, inverses; abelian if commutative; subgroup H ≤ G; Lagrange: |H| divides |G|.
Notes: group (G,·): closure + associativity + identity + inverses; abelian = commutative; Lagrange: |H| divides |G|

Concept: The binomial theorem: (a+b)^n = Σₖ C(n,k) aⁿ⁻ᵏ bᵏ; C(n,k) = n!/(k!(n-k)!); Pascal's triangle gives coefficients.
Notes: binomial theorem: (a+b)^n = Σ C(n,k) a^(n-k) b^k; C(n,k)=n!/k!(n-k)!; Pascal's triangle = coefficients

Concept: L'Hôpital's rule: if lim f(x)/g(x) is 0/0 or ∞/∞, then lim f(x)/g(x) = lim f'(x)/g'(x); apply repeatedly if still indeterminate.
Notes: L'Hôpital: 0/0 or ∞/∞ → lim f/g = lim f'/g'; apply repeatedly if still indeterminate

Concept: The Pythagorean triples: integer solutions to a² + b² = c²; primitive triples via m>n: a=m²-n², b=2mn, c=m²+n²; smallest (3,4,5).
Notes: Pythagorean triples: a²+b²=c²; primitive via m>n: a=m²-n², b=2mn, c=m²+n²; (3,4,5) smallest

Concept: Topology: continuous deformations preserve topological properties; homeomorphism = bijective continuous map w/ continuous inverse; homotopy equivalence is coarser.
Notes: topology: continuous deformations preserve topological props; homeomorphism = bijective + continuous both ways

Concept: The Möbius strip: non-orientable surface; one side, one edge; constructed by twisting strip 180° and joining ends; Klein bottle = non-orientable closed surface.
Notes: Möbius strip: non-orientable; 1 side, 1 edge; 180° twist + join; Klein bottle = non-orientable closed surface

Concept: Number theory: Fermat's little theorem: a^p ≡ a (mod p) for prime p; Euler's theorem: a^φ(n) ≡ 1 (mod n) for gcd(a,n)=1; basis of RSA.
Notes: Fermat's little: a^p ≡ a (mod p); Euler's: a^φ(n)≡1(mod n) if gcd(a,n)=1; basis of RSA

Concept: Probability density function: ∫f(x)dx = 1 over all x; E[X] = ∫xf(x)dx; Var[X] = E[X²] - (E[X])²; CDF F(x) = P(X ≤ x).
Notes: PDF: ∫f(x)dx=1; E[X]=∫xf(x)dx; Var[X]=E[X²]-(E[X])²; CDF F(x)=P(X≤x)

Concept: Eigendecomposition: A = PΛP⁻¹ where Λ = diagonal eigenvalue matrix, P = eigenvector matrix; requires distinct eigenvalues or diagonalizable matrix.
Notes: eigendecomposition: A=PΛP⁻¹; Λ=diag eigenvalues; P=eigenvectors; requires diagonalizable A

Concept: The triangle inequality: |a + b| ≤ |a| + |b|; equality iff a and b same direction; generalizes to metric spaces d(x,z) ≤ d(x,y) + d(y,z).
Notes: triangle inequality: |a+b| ≤ |a|+|b|; equality iff same direction; metric spaces: d(x,z)≤d(x,y)+d(y,z)

Concept: Fourier series: periodic function f(x) = a₀/2 + Σ(aₙcos(nωx) + bₙsin(nωx)); aₙ = (2/T)∫f(x)cos(nωx)dx; decomposes signal into harmonics.
Notes: Fourier series: f(x) = Σaₙcos(nωx)+bₙsin(nωx); aₙ=(2/T)∫f·cos dx; decomposes periodic signal into harmonics

Concept: The normal distribution: f(x) = (1/σ√2π) e^(-(x-μ)²/2σ²); 68-95-99.7 rule; standardize z = (x-μ)/σ; sum of normals is normal.
Notes: normal dist: f(x)=(1/σ√2π)e^(-(x-μ)²/2σ²); 68-95-99.7 rule; z=(x-μ)/σ; sum of normals = normal

Concept: Conditional probability: P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B); independent events: P(A|B) = P(A); law of total probability: P(A) = ΣP(A|Bᵢ)P(Bᵢ).
Notes: conditional prob: P(A|B)=P(A∩B)/P(B); independent: P(A|B)=P(A); total prob: P(A)=ΣP(A|Bᵢ)P(Bᵢ)

Concept: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Part 1: d/dx ∫ₐˣ f(t)dt = f(x); differentiating an integral returns the integrand evaluated at upper limit.
Notes: FTC Part 1: d/dx ∫ₐˣ f(t)dt = f(x); differentiating integral → integrand at upper limit

Concept: Linear programming: optimize cᵀx subject to Ax ≤ b, x ≥ 0; optimal solution at vertex of feasible polytope; solved by simplex method or interior point methods.
Notes: linear programming: minimize cᵀx s.t. Ax≤b, x≥0; optimal at vertex; simplex or interior point methods

Concept: The Euler characteristic χ = V - E + F for polyhedra; for sphere χ=2; torus χ=0; non-orientable surface χ < 0; topological invariant.
Notes: Euler characteristic: χ = V-E+F; sphere χ=2; torus χ=0; topological invariant

Concept: Set theory: De Morgan's laws: (A∪B)ᶜ = Aᶜ∩Bᶜ; (A∩B)ᶜ = Aᶜ∪Bᶜ; complement of union = intersection of complements; applies to logic (not(A or B) = not A and not B).
Notes: De Morgan: (A∪B)ᶜ=Aᶜ∩Bᶜ; (A∩B)ᶜ=Aᶜ∪Bᶜ; logic: ¬(A∨B)=¬A∧¬B; ¬(A∧B)=¬A∨¬B

Concept: Permutations and combinations: P(n,r) = n!/(n-r)! ordered selections; C(n,r) = n!/(r!(n-r)!) unordered; C(n,r) = C(n,n-r) symmetry.
Notes: permutations: P(n,r)=n!/(n-r)! (ordered); combinations: C(n,r)=n!/r!(n-r)! (unordered); C(n,r)=C(n,n-r)

Concept: Integration by parts: ∫u dv = uv - ∫v du; choose u by LIATE order (Logs, Inverse trig, Algebraic, Trig, Exponential); reduces integral complexity.
Notes: integration by parts: ∫u dv = uv - ∫v du; LIATE for choosing u; reduces complexity

Concept: The squeeze theorem: if g(x) ≤ f(x) ≤ h(x) and lim g(x) = lim h(x) = L, then lim f(x) = L; used to prove lim sin(x)/x = 1 as x→0.
Notes: squeeze theorem: g≤f≤h and lim g=lim h=L → lim f=L; proves lim sin(x)/x=1 as x→0

Concept: Abstract algebra: a ring has addition (abelian group) + multiplication (associative, distributive); field = commutative ring w/ multiplicative inverses for non-zero elements.
Notes: ring: abelian group under + with associative distributive ×; field = commutative ring + ×-inverses for nonzero

Concept: The Gaussian integral: ∫₋∞^∞ e^(-x²) dx = √π; ∫₋∞^∞ e^(-ax²) dx = √(π/a); derived via polar coordinate trick; central to probability and quantum mechanics.
Notes: Gaussian integral: ∫e^(-x²)dx=√π; ∫e^(-ax²)dx=√(π/a); polar coord trick; key in prob + QM

Concept: Generating functions: transform sequence (a₀, a₁, ...) to power series A(x) = Σaₙxⁿ; multiplication of GFs = convolution of sequences; useful in combinatorics.
Notes: generating functions: seq → A(x)=Σaₙxⁿ; GF multiplication = sequence convolution; combinatorics tool

Concept: Number bases: binary (base-2) used in computing; hexadecimal (base-16) compact binary representation; convert decimal to binary by successive division by 2.
Notes: number bases: binary (base-2, computing); hex (base-16, compact binary); decimal→binary: successive ÷2 w/ remainders

Concept: The median: value where half the data lies above and half below; more robust to outliers than mean; calculated as middle value (odd n) or average of two middle (even n).
Notes: median: midpoint of sorted data; robust to outliers (vs mean); odd n=middle; even n=avg of 2 middle

Concept: Lagrange multipliers: optimize f(x,y) subject to g(x,y)=c; condition ∇f = λ∇g; solve system with constraint; λ is shadow price in economics.
Notes: Lagrange multipliers: optimize f s.t. g=c; ∇f=λ∇g + constraint; λ=shadow price

Concept: Expected value linearity: E[aX+bY] = aE[X]+bE[Y] regardless of dependence; E[XY] = E[X]E[Y] only if X,Y independent; useful for variance calculation.
Notes: E[aX+bY]=aE[X]+bE[Y] (linearity always); E[XY]=E[X]E[Y] only if independent; useful for Var[X+Y]

Concept: The inclusion-exclusion principle: |A∪B| = |A| + |B| - |A∩B|; for three sets: |A∪B∪C| = |A|+|B|+|C| - |A∩B| - |A∩C| - |B∩C| + |A∩B∩C|.
Notes: inclusion-exclusion: |A∪B|=|A|+|B|-|A∩B|; 3 sets: sum singles - sum pairs + triple intersection

Concept: Numerical methods: Newton-Raphson root finding: xₙ₊₁ = xₙ - f(xₙ)/f'(xₙ); quadratic convergence near root; requires differentiable f and good initial guess.
Notes: Newton-Raphson: xₙ₊₁=xₙ-f(xₙ)/f'(xₙ); quadratic convergence; requires differentiable f + good init guess

Concept: Random variables: discrete (probability mass function), continuous (PDF); variance Var[X] = E[(X-μ)²] = E[X²]-μ²; standard deviation σ = √Var[X].
Notes: RV: discrete=PMF; continuous=PDF; Var[X]=E[(X-μ)²]=E[X²]-μ²; σ=√Var[X]

Concept: The multiplier effect: an initial spending increase of $X leads to a total increase in GDP of $X/(1-MPC); if MPC=0.8, multiplier=5.
Notes: multiplier: ΔY = ΔSpending × 1/(1-MPC); MPC=0.8 → multiplier=5; Keynesian fiscal mechanism

Concept: Rent-seeking behavior: firms/individuals expend resources to capture existing wealth rather than create new value; lobbying, regulatory capture; deadweight loss.
Notes: rent-seeking: resources spent capturing wealth ≠ creating value; lobbying/regulatory capture → deadweight loss

Concept: The Heckscher-Ohlin model predicts trade patterns based on factor endowments; capital-abundant countries export capital-intensive goods; labor-abundant export labor-intensive.
Notes: Heckscher-Ohlin: trade ∝ factor endowments; capital-abundant → export capital-intensive; labor-abundant → export labor-intensive

Concept: Public goods: non-rivalrous (one use doesn't diminish for others) + non-excludable (can't prevent free-riding); undersupplied by markets; government provision justified.
Notes: public goods: non-rivalrous + non-excludable; free-rider problem → market undersupply; ∴ govt provision justified

Concept: The theory of second best: if one Pareto optimality condition cannot be satisfied, it is not necessarily desirable to satisfy others; piecemeal policy reforms may worsen welfare.
Notes: 2nd best theory: if 1 Pareto condition fails, satisfying others may worsen welfare; piecemeal reforms dangerous

Concept: Monopolistic competition: many firms, differentiated products, free entry/exit; short-run profits → entry until zero profits; excess capacity; product variety valued by consumers.
Notes: monopolistic competition: many firms + differentiated products + free entry; SR profits → entry → LR zero profit; excess capacity

Concept: The Coase theorem: if property rights are well-defined and transaction costs are zero, private bargaining leads to efficient outcomes regardless of initial rights assignment.
Notes: Coase theorem: well-defined property rights + zero transaction costs → efficient private bargaining; initial assignment irrelevant

Concept: Hyperinflation occurs when monthly inflation exceeds 50%; destroys store-of-value function of money; caused by excessive money printing; historical examples: Weimar, Zimbabwe.
Notes: hyperinflation: >50% monthly inflation; destroys money's store-of-value; cause: excessive money printing; Weimar/Zimbabwe

Concept: The Okun's law: for every 1% unemployment above natural rate, GDP is ~2% below potential; inverse relationship between unemployment gap and output gap.
Notes: Okun's law: 1% ↑ unemployment above natural rate → ~2% ↓ GDP below potential; inverse U-output gap relationship

Concept: Externalities: negative externality → private cost < social cost → overproduction; positive externality → private benefit < social benefit → underproduction; Pigou tax/subsidy solution.
Notes: externalities: negative (private cost < social cost → overproduction); positive (private benefit < social → underproduction); Pigou tax/subsidy

Concept: The velocity of money: MV = PQ (quantity theory); M = money supply, V = velocity, P = price level, Q = real output; ↑M → ↑P if V, Q constant.
Notes: quantity theory: MV=PQ; ↑M → ↑P if V+Q constant; velocity V = nominal GDP/M

Concept: Price discrimination: 1st degree (perfect, different price per unit); 2nd degree (quantity discounts); 3rd degree (different price per market segment, e.g., student discounts).
Notes: price discrimination: 1st = different price per unit (perfect); 2nd = quantity discounts; 3rd = market segments (e.g., student)

Concept: The Ricardian model of trade: comparative advantage based on relative labor productivity; even a less productive country benefits from specializing in its least-bad good.
Notes: Ricardian trade: comparative advantage = relative labor productivity; specialize in least-bad good; both countries benefit

Concept: Search and matching models: unemployment from matching frictions between job seekers and vacancies; Beveridge curve: inverse relationship between unemployment and vacancies.
Notes: search+matching: unemployment from friction; Beveridge curve: ↑ vacancies ↔ ↓ unemployment; matching efficiency

Concept: The Cobb-Douglas production function: Y = AK^α L^(1-α); constant returns to scale; elasticity of output w.r.t. capital = α; labor share = 1-α if competitive markets.
Notes: Cobb-Douglas: Y=AK^α L^(1-α); CRS; ∂Y/∂K elasticity=α; labor share=1-α (competitive)

Concept: Moral hazard in insurance: insured parties take more risk knowing they're protected; reduced by deductibles, copayments, monitoring; health insurance and banking examples.
Notes: moral hazard: insurance → ↑ risk-taking; fix: deductibles + copayments + monitoring; health/banking examples

Concept: The demand for money (Keynes): transaction motive (proportional to income); precautionary (buffer); speculative (inverse of interest rate); Md = L(Y, i).
Notes: money demand (Keynes): transaction motive (∝Y) + precautionary + speculative (inverse to i); Md=L(Y,i)

Concept: The natural monopoly problem: declining average cost curve means single firm serves market cheaper than multiple firms; solution: regulation or public ownership.
Notes: natural monopoly: ↓ average cost → single firm cheapest; solution: regulate (price = AC) or public ownership

Concept: Intertemporal budget constraint: C₁ + C₂/(1+r) = Y₁ + Y₂/(1+r); present value of consumption = present value of income; ↑r → substitution effect (save more) + income effect.
Notes: intertemporal budget: C₁ + C₂/(1+r) = Y₁ + Y₂/(1+r); ↑r → substitution effect (save more) + income effect

Concept: The Solow residual (TFP): growth unexplained by labor and capital inputs; measured as: TFP growth = output growth - α·capital growth - (1-α)·labor growth.
Notes: Solow residual (TFP): unexplained growth; TFP growth = output growth - α·K growth - (1-α)·L growth

Concept: Giffen goods: inferior goods with very strong income effect; demand rises when price rises; potatoes during Irish famine; violates normal demand law.
Notes: Giffen goods: inferior + strong income effect → demand ↑ when price ↑; Irish famine potatoes; violates normal demand

Concept: The Kaldor-Hicks efficiency criterion: a policy is efficient if gainers could compensate losers; weaker than Pareto; justifies policies with losers if net gain > 0.
Notes: Kaldor-Hicks: efficient if gainers could compensate losers; weaker than Pareto; net gain > 0 justifies policy

Concept: Rational expectations hypothesis: economic agents form expectations using all available information and the correct model; systematic errors impossible; Lucas critique implication.
Notes: rational expectations: agents use all available info + correct model; no systematic errors; → Lucas critique

Concept: The Laffer curve: tax revenue = 0 at 0% rate and 100% rate; peak at some optimal rate; basis of supply-side argument that ↓ tax rates can ↑ revenue.
Notes: Laffer curve: revenue=0 at 0% and 100% tax; peak in between; supply-side: ↓ tax rate may ↑ revenue

Concept: Stagflation (1970s) was caused by oil supply shocks shifting aggregate supply left; resulted in simultaneous ↑ inflation + ↑ unemployment; challenged Phillips curve.
Notes: stagflation: supply shock (OPEC oil) → AS shifts left → ↑ inflation + ↑ unemployment; challenged Phillips curve

Concept: The principal-agent problem: separation of ownership (principal) and control (agent); agent may maximize own utility; solved by incentive contracts, monitoring, bonding.
Notes: principal-agent: owner ≠ manager interests; agent self-interested; fix: incentive contracts + monitoring + bonding

Concept: Endogenous growth theory: R&D, human capital, and knowledge spillovers generate sustained growth internally; ∴ growth policy can have permanent level effects.
Notes: endogenous growth: R&D + human capital + knowledge spillovers → sustained growth internally; policy → permanent effects

Concept: The theory of comparative advantage applies even when one country is absolutely better at everything; specialize based on relative opportunity costs, not absolute productivity.
Notes: comparative advantage: specialize on relative opportunity cost even if absolutely worse; trade benefits both parties

Concept: Currency crisis: speculative attack on fixed exchange rate when reserves insufficient to defend peg; first-generation model (fiscal deficits); second-generation (self-fulfilling).
Notes: currency crisis: speculative attack on peg; 1st gen = fiscal deficits drain reserves; 2nd gen = self-fulfilling expectations

Concept: Asymmetric information in credit markets: adverse selection (high-risk borrowers more likely to seek loans) + moral hazard (borrowers take more risk w/ OPM); → credit rationing.
Notes: credit markets: adverse selection (risky borrowers self-select) + moral hazard (OPM risk-taking) → credit rationing

Concept: The efficient wage hypothesis: firms pay above market-clearing wages to reduce shirking, attract quality workers, and reduce turnover; explains involuntary unemployment.
Notes: efficiency wages: pay above market-clearing → ↓ shirking + ↑ worker quality + ↓ turnover; explains involuntary unemployment

Concept: Monetary policy transmission: ↓ policy rate → ↓ bank lending rates → ↑ investment + consumption → ↑ GDP + ↑ inflation; exchange rate channel → ↑ exports.
Notes: monetary transmission: ↓ rate → ↓ lending rates → ↑ investment+consumption → ↑ GDP+inflation; FX channel → ↑ exports

Concept: The prisoner's dilemma: dominant strategy = defect for both; Nash equilibrium = (Defect, Defect); collectively worse than (Cooperate, Cooperate); resolved by repetition.
Notes: prisoner's dilemma: dominant strategy = defect for both → (D,D) Nash eq; collectively suboptimal; repetition → cooperation

Concept: Diminishing marginal utility: each additional unit of a good provides less utility than the previous; implies convex indifference curves; basis of insurance demand.
Notes: diminishing marginal utility: each extra unit → less utility; → convex indifference curves; basis of insurance demand

Concept: Transfer pricing: multinational firms set prices for intra-firm transactions across borders; can shift profits to low-tax jurisdictions; OECD arm's-length standard as response.
Notes: transfer pricing: intra-firm prices across borders; profit shifting to low-tax jurisdictions; OECD arm's-length standard = fix

Concept: International trade: gravity model predicts bilateral trade ∝ product of GDPs and ∝ 1/distance; borders, language, and cultural differences reduce trade.
Notes: gravity model: trade ∝ GDP₁×GDP₂/distance; ↓ borders/language/culture barriers → ↑ trade

Concept: The fiscal multiplier is smaller (approaching 1 or below) in open economies, when monetary policy accommodates, and when output gap is small; larger during recessions.
Notes: fiscal multiplier: smaller in open economies + when MP accommodates + near full employment; larger during recessions

Concept: Behavioral economics: systematic deviations from rationality include loss aversion (~2×), present bias (hyperbolic discounting), anchoring, availability heuristic.
Notes: behavioral econ: loss aversion ~2×; present bias (hyperbolic discounting); anchoring; availability heuristic; systematic irrationality

Concept: The Mundell-Fleming model in open economy: fiscal policy effective under fixed FX (crowding-in via capital inflows); monetary policy effective under floating FX.
Notes: Mundell-Fleming: fixed FX → fiscal policy effective (capital inflows); floating FX → monetary policy effective

Concept: Torts: negligence requires duty of care + breach + causation (but-for + proximate) + damages; res ipsa loquitur infers negligence from circumstances.
Notes: negligence: duty + breach + causation (but-for + proximate) + damages; res ipsa loquitur = inferred negligence

Concept: The parol evidence rule: written contract representing final agreement cannot be contradicted by prior oral/written agreements; exceptions: fraud, ambiguity, condition precedent.
Notes: parol evidence rule: final written contract bars prior oral/written contradictions; exceptions: fraud, ambiguity, condition precedent

Concept: Copyright protects original expression (not ideas) automatically upon creation; duration = life + 70 years; fair use defense: purpose, nature, amount, market effect.
Notes: copyright: protects expression (not ideas); automatic at creation; duration=life+70 yrs; fair use: purpose+nature+amount+market effect

Concept: Strict liability: defendant liable without proof of negligence; applies to abnormally dangerous activities, products liability (manufacturing defects), wild animals.
Notes: strict liability: no negligence needed; abnormally dangerous activities + products (mfg defects) + wild animals

Concept: The Chevron doctrine: courts defer to agency's reasonable interpretation of ambiguous statute; Step 1 = is statute clear?; Step 2 = is interpretation reasonable?
Notes: Chevron: Step 1 = statute clear? → apply it; Step 2 = ambiguous → agency interpretation reasonable? → defer

Concept: Equity of redemption: mortgagor's right to redeem property by paying off debt even after default; equitable mortgage prevents unconscionable foreclosure.
Notes: equity of redemption: mortgagor can redeem property after default by paying debt; equity prevents unconscionable foreclosure

Concept: Tortious interference: intentional, improper interference with another's contract or business relationship; must prove existence of contract, knowledge, intentional inducement, damages.
Notes: tortious interference: prove contract existence + defendant's knowledge + intentional inducement + damages

Concept: The doctrine of unconscionability: courts may refuse to enforce contracts that are oppressively one-sided; procedural (bargaining unfairness) + substantive (terms).
Notes: unconscionability: procedural (unfair bargaining) + substantive (oppressive terms) → court may void contract

Concept: The Foreign Sovereign Immunities Act: foreign governments immune from US court jurisdiction unless exceptions apply (commercial activity, tort in US, waiver).
Notes: FSIA: foreign govts immune from US courts; exceptions: commercial activity + tort in US + waiver

Concept: Promissory note vs. mortgage: note = personal promise to repay; mortgage = security interest in real property; foreclosure = remedy on default; non-recourse limits liability to collateral.
Notes: promissory note = personal promise; mortgage = security in realty; foreclosure on default; non-recourse = liability limited to collateral

Concept: The Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) Article 2 governs sale of goods; merchants have higher standard; acceptance can differ from offer (battle of the forms).
Notes: UCC Art 2: sale of goods; merchants = higher standard; battle of forms: acceptance can differ from offer

Concept: Comparative fault (pure): plaintiff recovery reduced by own fault percentage; e.g., 30% at fault → recovers 70% of damages; all defendants share liability proportionally.
Notes: comparative fault (pure): recovery = damages × (1 - plaintiff's fault %); 30% at fault → 70% recovery

Concept: The dormant Commerce Clause prevents states from discriminating against or placing undue burdens on interstate commerce; three-part Pike balancing test.
Notes: dormant Commerce Clause: states cannot discriminate against interstate commerce; Pike test: state interest vs burden

Concept: International law sources: treaties, customary international law (general practice + opinio juris), general principles, judicial decisions, scholarly writings.
Notes: intl law sources: treaties + customary intl law (practice + opinio juris) + general principles + judicial decisions + scholarship

Concept: The Fourth Amendment exclusionary rule: Mapp v. Ohio applied to states via 14th Amendment; good faith exception (US v. Leon); inevitable discovery doctrine.
Notes: exclusionary rule: Mapp v. Ohio (state application); good faith exception (Leon); inevitable discovery doctrine

Concept: Labor law: National Labor Relations Act protects collective bargaining; NLRB adjudicates unfair labor practices; Section 7 rights = organize, collectively bargain, strike.
Notes: NLRA: protects collective bargaining; NLRB adjudicates ULPs; Section 7: organize + bargain + strike rights

Concept: Securitization: pool assets (mortgages, loans) → special purpose vehicle (SPV) → issue tranched securities; senior tranche paid first; moral hazard in originate-to-distribute.
Notes: securitization: assets → SPV → tranched securities; senior tranche = paid first; originate-to-distribute → moral hazard

Concept: The rule of lenity: ambiguous criminal statutes interpreted in favor of the defendant; prevents prosecutorial abuse; applies only after other interpretation tools exhausted.
Notes: rule of lenity: ambiguous criminal statute → interpret in defendant's favor; last resort after other tools fail

Concept: Absolute liability vs. strict liability: strict = no fault needed but reasonable precautions may be defense; absolute = no defenses whatsoever; rare.
Notes: strict liability: no fault needed; precautions may be defense; absolute liability: no defenses; more extreme than strict

Concept: The doctrine of promissory estoppel elements: promise + reasonable reliance + detriment + injustice if not enforced; substitute for consideration in equity.
Notes: promissory estoppel: promise + reasonable reliance + detriment + injustice to not enforce; substitute for consideration

Concept: Regulatory taking: government regulation that goes too far = taking requiring compensation under 5th Amendment; Penn Central test: economic impact + investment-backed expectations + character of action.
Notes: regulatory taking: regulation → compensation if too far; Penn Central: economic impact + investment expectations + character

Concept: Vicarious liability: employer liable for employee torts committed within scope of employment; independent contractors generally excluded; non-delegable duties exception.
Notes: vicarious liability: employer liable for employee torts (scope of employment); independent contractors excluded; non-delegable duties exception

Concept: Securities law: Rule 10b-5 prohibits fraud in securities transactions; insider trading requires material non-public information + breach of duty + trading; Dirks test for tippee liability.
Notes: Rule 10b-5: prohibit securities fraud; insider trading = material non-public info + duty breach + trade; Dirks = tippee liability

Concept: The economic loss rule: tort law does not compensate for pure economic losses absent physical injury or property damage; exceptions for negligent misrepresentation, professional malpractice.
Notes: economic loss rule: no tort recovery for pure economic loss w/o physical injury; exceptions: negligent misrepresentation, malpractice

Concept: Trademark protection: distinctive marks (fanciful > arbitrary > suggestive > descriptive > generic); dilution by blurring or tarnishment; international protection via Madrid Protocol.
Notes: trademark: fanciful>arbitrary>suggestive>descriptive>generic; dilution: blurring or tarnishment; Madrid Protocol = intl

Concept: Contract formation: offer + acceptance + consideration + capacity + legality; counteroffers terminate original offer; mirror image rule vs. UCC battle of forms.
Notes: contract: offer + acceptance + consideration + capacity + legality; counteroffer terminates offer; mirror image rule vs. UCC battle of forms

Concept: The business judgment rule: directors presumed to act in good faith, informed, and in corporation's best interest; rebuttable; overcome by showing fraud or gross negligence.
Notes: BJR: directors presumed good faith + informed + corp's interest; rebuttable by fraud or gross negligence

Concept: Substantive due process: government may not deprive persons of fundamental rights w/o compelling interest + narrow tailoring; non-fundamental rights = rational basis review.
Notes: substantive due process: fundamental rights → compelling interest + narrow tailoring; non-fundamental → rational basis

Concept: The Clean Air Act: EPA sets National Ambient Air Quality Standards (NAAQS); states implement via SIPs; stationary sources need permits; citizen suit provision.
Notes: Clean Air Act: EPA sets NAAQS; states implement via SIPs; stationary source permits; citizen suit provision

Concept: Fraudulent conveyance: transfer of assets to hinder creditors; Uniform Fraudulent Transfer Act: actual intent fraud or constructive fraud (insolvent + inadequate consideration).
Notes: fraudulent conveyance: transfer to hinder creditors; UFTA: actual intent or constructive (insolvent + inadequate consideration)

Concept: Working memory model (Baddeley): central executive + phonological loop (verbal) + visuospatial sketchpad (visual) + episodic buffer (integrated storage); ~4 chunks capacity.
Notes: working memory (Baddeley): central executive + phonological loop + visuospatial sketchpad + episodic buffer; ~4 chunks

Concept: Hebbian plasticity: 'neurons that fire together wire together'; correlated activity → LTP; uncorrelated or anti-correlated → LTD; basis of associative learning.
Notes: Hebbian: fire together → wire together; correlated → LTP; uncorrelated/anti-correlated → LTD; associative learning basis

Concept: The dual-process theory (Kahneman): System 1 = fast, automatic, intuitive, unconscious; System 2 = slow, deliberate, effortful, conscious; cognitive biases from System 1.
Notes: dual-process (Kahneman): S1=fast+automatic+intuitive; S2=slow+deliberate+effortful; cognitive biases = S1 errors

Concept: Operant conditioning: positive reinforcement (add reward → ↑ behavior); negative reinforcement (remove aversive → ↑ behavior); punishment (↓ behavior); extinction (no response → behavior stops).
Notes: operant conditioning: +reinforce (add reward→↑beh); -reinforce (remove aversive→↑beh); punishment (↓beh); extinction (no response→beh stops)

Concept: The Big Five personality traits (OCEAN): Openness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Agreeableness, Neuroticism; stable across cultures; predict job performance and health outcomes.
Notes: Big Five (OCEAN): Openness+Conscientiousness+Extraversion+Agreeableness+Neuroticism; culturally stable; predicts job+health

Concept: Attachment theory (Bowlby/Ainsworth): secure (confident exploration), anxious-avoidant, anxious-ambivalent, disorganized; early attachment patterns shape adult relationships.
Notes: attachment (Bowlby/Ainsworth): secure (explore freely) + anxious-avoidant + anxious-ambivalent + disorganized; early patterns → adult relationships

Concept: The self-determination theory: competence + autonomy + relatedness are basic psychological needs; intrinsic motivation > extrinsic for sustained behavior; overjustification effect.
Notes: SDT: competence + autonomy + relatedness = basic needs; intrinsic > extrinsic for sustained beh; overjustification effect

Concept: Maslow's hierarchy: physiological → safety → love/belonging → esteem → self-actualization; lower needs must be met before higher; peak experiences at top.
Notes: Maslow: physiological→safety→love/belonging→esteem→self-actualization; lower needs first; peak experience at top

Concept: The prefrontal cortex: dorsolateral PFC = working memory + planning; ventromedial PFC = emotion regulation + decision-making; orbitofrontal = reward + social cognition.
Notes: PFC: dorsolateral = working memory + planning; ventromedial = emotion regulation + decisions; orbitofrontal = reward + social cognition

Concept: Pavlovian fear conditioning: CS (tone) paired w/ US (shock) → CR (freezing); amygdala (basolateral) acquires; infralimbic cortex extinguishes; NMDA receptors required.
Notes: fear conditioning: CS(tone)+US(shock)→CR(freezing); BLA acquires; infralimbic extinguishes; NMDA required

Concept: The Stroop effect: naming ink color of incongruent color words is slower; selective attention + cognitive control required; measures executive function; frontal lobe involvement.
Notes: Stroop effect: naming ink color of color words (incongruent) → slower; selective attention + executive control; frontal lobe

Concept: Phantom limb pain: pain perceived in amputated limb; caused by cortical remapping and maladaptive plasticity; treated with mirror therapy, virtual reality, graded motor imagery.
Notes: phantom limb pain: pain in amputated limb; cortical remapping + maladaptive plasticity; tx: mirror therapy/VR/graded motor imagery

Concept: The mesolimbic dopamine system: VTA → nucleus accumbens; encodes reward prediction error (RPE = actual - predicted reward); depletion causes anhedonia.
Notes: mesolimbic DA: VTA → nucleus accumbens; encodes RPE (actual - predicted reward); ↓ DA → anhedonia

Concept: Cognitive behavioral model of depression: negative cognitive triad (self, world, future) + cognitive distortions (catastrophizing, black-and-white thinking); treated with CBT.
Notes: CBT depression model: negative triad (self+world+future) + cognitive distortions (catastrophizing, B&W thinking); CBT targets distortions

Concept: The default mode network deactivates during focused external tasks; activated by self-referential thought, mind-wandering, perspective-taking; key node: medial PFC, PCC.
Notes: DMN: deactivates during external focus; active: self-referential thought, mind-wandering, perspective-taking; mPFC + PCC key nodes

Concept: Erikson's psychosocial stages: trust vs. mistrust (infancy) → autonomy vs. shame → initiative vs. guilt → industry vs. inferiority → identity vs. role confusion → intimacy vs. isolation → generativity → integrity vs. despair.
Notes: Erikson stages: trust→autonomy→initiative→industry→identity→intimacy→generativity→integrity; each = psychosocial conflict

Concept: Emotional regulation strategies: reappraisal (effective, changes interpretation before emotion) vs. suppression (ineffective, hides emotion after); reappraisal → better outcomes.
Notes: emotion regulation: reappraisal (change interpretation early) = effective; suppression (hide after) = ineffective; reappraisal → better health+relationships

Concept: Theories of consciousness: Global Workspace Theory (Baars): conscious = broadcast to many brain areas; Integrated Information Theory (Tononi): consciousness = Phi (information integration).
Notes: consciousness theories: GWT (Baars) = conscious info broadcast widely; IIT (Tononi) = consciousness ∝ Phi (integrated info)

Concept: The reinforcement learning model: agent takes action in environment → receives reward → updates value function; Q-learning: Q(s,a) ← Q(s,a) + α[r + γ·max Q(s',a') - Q(s,a)].
Notes: RL: agent → action → reward → update value; Q-learning: Q(s,a) ← Q+α[r+γ·maxQ(s')-Q]; TD learning

Concept: The James-Lange theory of emotion: physiological arousal precedes and causes emotion; stimulus → body response → conscious feeling; Cannon-Bard: simultaneous arousal + feeling.
Notes: James-Lange: stimulus → body arousal → emotion; arousal precedes feeling; Cannon-Bard: arousal + feeling simultaneous

Concept: The curse of knowledge: once you know something, difficult to imagine not knowing it; leads to communication failures, poor teaching; children = less affected.
Notes: curse of knowledge: knowing X → hard to imagine not knowing X; communication failure; poor teaching; ↓ empathy for novices

Concept: The serial position effect: better recall for first (primacy = long-term memory) and last items (recency = working memory); middle items poorest recall.
Notes: serial position: primacy (first items = LTM) + recency (last items = WM) → best recall; middle = worst

Concept: Autistic spectrum disorder: impaired social communication + restricted/repetitive behaviors; genetic component (~80%); structural differences in amygdala, cerebellum, mirror neuron system.
Notes: ASD: ↓ social communication + restricted/repetitive behavior; ~80% genetic; amygdala+cerebellum+mirror neuron diffs

Concept: The cocktail party effect: ability to selectively attend to one voice in noisy environment; early selection (Broadbent) vs. late selection (Deutsch-Norman) models of attention.
Notes: cocktail party effect: selective attention to one voice; early selection (Broadbent) vs. late selection (Deutsch-Norman)

Concept: Psychopharmacology: drug efficacy = Emax (max effect); potency = ED50 (dose for half-max effect); therapeutic index TI = LD50/ED50; wide TI = safer drug.
Notes: drug: Emax = max effect; ED50 = dose for half-max (potency); therapeutic index = LD50/ED50; ↑ TI = safer

Concept: The spreading activation model: semantic memory as network; activating a concept spreads to related concepts; explains priming effects and semantic interference.
Notes: spreading activation: semantic network; activating node → spreads to related nodes; explains priming + semantic interference

Concept: Flow state (Csikszentmihalyi): optimal experience when challenge slightly exceeds skill; full absorption; intrinsic reward; anxiety if challenge >> skill; boredom if skill >> challenge.
Notes: flow (Csikszentmihalyi): challenge slightly > skill → absorption + intrinsic reward; anxiety: challenge>>skill; boredom: skill>>challenge

Concept: The somatosensory cortex: primary S1 (postcentral gyrus) processes touch, pressure, pain, temperature; secondary S2 (parietal operculum) integrates + identifies; proportional to receptor density.
Notes: S1 (postcentral gyrus): touch/pressure/pain/temp; S2 (parietal operculum): integration + ID; cortical area ∝ receptor density

Concept: Social identity theory (Tajfel): group membership → social identity; in-group favoritism + out-group derogation; minimal group paradigm shows arbitrary groups sufficient.
Notes: social identity theory: group membership → identity; in-group favoritism + out-group derogation; minimal group: arbitrary groups sufficient

Concept: The neuroscience of pain: A-delta fibers (sharp, fast, well-localized pain); C-fibers (dull, slow, diffuse); gate control theory (Melzack-Wall): non-painful stimuli close gate.
Notes: pain: Aδ fibers = sharp/fast/localized; C fibers = dull/slow/diffuse; gate control (Melzack-Wall): non-pain stim closes gate

Concept: Confirmation bias: tendency to search for, interpret, and recall information confirming pre-existing beliefs; driven by motivated reasoning; ↑ in polarized environments.
Notes: confirmation bias: seek+interpret+recall info confirming existing beliefs; motivated reasoning; ↑ in polarized environments

Concept: The basal ganglia: direct pathway (D1, facilitates movement); indirect pathway (D2, inhibits movement); they balance to fine-tune motor output; substantia nigra provides dopamine.
Notes: basal ganglia: direct pathway (D1 → movement facilitation); indirect (D2 → inhibition); balance = fine-tune motor; SN provides DA

Concept: Milgram obedience experiments: 65% of participants administered maximum shocks when ordered by authority figure; situational factors > character; ethical concerns sparked IRB rules.
Notes: Milgram obedience: 65% gave max shocks under authority; situation > character; sparked IRB ethics rules

Concept: The broaden-and-build theory (Fredrickson): positive emotions broaden thought-action repertoires; build lasting resources (social, cognitive, physical); buffer against stress.
Notes: broaden-and-build (Fredrickson): +emotions → broaden thought repertoire → build lasting resources; buffer against stress

Concept: Neuroinflammation: activated microglia + astrocytes release pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-1β, TNF-α); contributes to neurodegenerative diseases (Alzheimer's, Parkinson's); bidirectional w/ depression.
Notes: neuroinflammation: activated microglia+astrocytes → IL-1β/TNF-α; contributes to AD/PD; ↔ depression

Concept: The thalamus: sensory relay station (all senses except olfaction); pulvinar (visual attention); mediodorsal (PFC connections + executive function); intralaminar (consciousness/arousal).
Notes: thalamus: sensory relay (all except olfaction); pulvinar = visual attention; mediodorsal ↔ PFC; intralaminar = consciousness/arousal

Concept: Piaget's cognitive development: sensorimotor (0-2, object permanence) → preoperational (2-7, symbolic but egocentric) → concrete operational (7-11, conservation) → formal operational (11+, abstract).
Notes: Piaget: sensorimotor (0-2, object permanence) → preoperational (2-7, symbolic/egocentric) → concrete (7-11, conservation) → formal (11+, abstract)

Concept: Dissociation: disconnection between thoughts, feelings, and memories; depersonalization (feeling unreal); derealization (surroundings unreal); DID = extreme dissociation.
Notes: dissociation: thoughts/feelings/memories disconnect; depersonalization (self unreal); derealization (world unreal); DID = extreme form

Concept: The cocktail party and dichotic listening experiments: attended ear processed; unattended ear physically present not semantically analyzed (early filter) vs. partially (late filter).
Notes: dichotic listening: attended ear processed; unattended: early filter (Broadbent) = physical only; late filter = partial semantic processing

Concept: The Congress of Berlin (1878) redrew Balkan boundaries after Russo-Turkish War; Bismarck as 'honest broker'; left nationalism unresolved; contributed to 1914 powder keg.
Notes: Congress of Berlin 1878: Balkan redraw post-Russo-Turkish War; Bismarck honest broker; unresolved nationalism → 1914 powder keg

Concept: The Truman Doctrine (1947): US commitment to contain Soviet expansion; economic and military aid to Greece and Turkey; ideological framework for Cold War foreign policy.
Notes: Truman Doctrine 1947: contain Soviet expansion; aid to Greece+Turkey; Cold War ideological framework

Concept: The colonization of the Americas: Spanish encomienda system exploited indigenous labor; mission system forced conversion; smallpox decimated populations (90% in some regions).
Notes: Spanish Americas: encomienda = indigenous labor exploitation; missions = forced conversion; smallpox → 90% indigenous death in some regions

Concept: The French Revolution's phases: Constitutional Monarchy (1789-92) → First Republic (1792) → Reign of Terror (1793-94, Committee of Public Safety) → Directory (1795) → Napoleon.
Notes: French Rev phases: Constitutional Monarchy → Republic (1792) → Terror (1793-94, CPS) → Directory (1795) → Napoleon

Concept: The Seven Years' War (1756-63): first truly global war; Britain vs. France in Europe/Americas/India; Britain gains Canada and India; France loses empire; set stage for 1776.
Notes: Seven Years War 1756-63: global; Britain+Prussia vs France+Austria; Britain gains Canada+India; weakens France → sets up 1776

Concept: The Treaty of Tordesillas (1494): Portugal and Spain divided non-European world along meridian; Portugal = East (Africa, Brazil, India); Spain = West (most Americas).
Notes: Tordesillas 1494: Portugal+Spain divide world at meridian; Portugal = East (Africa/Brazil/India); Spain = West (Americas)

Concept: The Suez Crisis (1956): Nasser nationalizes Suez Canal; Britain, France, Israel attack; US and USSR pressure withdrawal; marked decline of British and French imperial power.
Notes: Suez Crisis 1956: Nasser nationalizes canal; Britain+France+Israel attack; US+USSR pressure withdrawal; signals end of British/French imperial power

Concept: The Boxer Rebellion (1900): Chinese uprising against foreign influence and Christian missionaries; 8-nation allied force crushed it; Boxer Protocol imposed reparations on China.
Notes: Boxer Rebellion 1900: Chinese uprising vs. foreign influence; 8-nation force crushed it; Boxer Protocol = reparations on China

Concept: The Reformation: Luther's 95 Theses (1517) challenged indulgences; printing press spread ideas; Council of Trent (1545-63) = Catholic Counter-Reformation; religious wars followed.
Notes: Reformation: Luther 95 Theses 1517 (indulgences); printing press spread; Council of Trent 1545-63 = Counter-Reformation; wars

Concept: The Congress of Vienna (1814-15): restored monarchies; created Concert of Europe; Metternich system; avoided general European war for 100 years until 1914.
Notes: Vienna 1814-15: restored monarchies; Concert of Europe (Metternich system); maintained European peace for ~100 years

Concept: The Korean War (1950-53): Kim Il-Sung invaded South; US-led UN forces intervened; China entered after Inchon; armistice at 38th parallel; technically ongoing; 36K US dead.
Notes: Korean War 1950-53: N. Korea invades; UN/US intervene; China enters post-Inchon; armistice at 38th parallel; technically ongoing; 36K US dead

Concept: The Opium Wars: First (1839-42) ended with Treaty of Nanking; Hong Kong ceded to Britain; extraterritoriality; unequal treaties humiliated China; century of humiliation begins.
Notes: First Opium War 1839-42: Treaty of Nanking; HK ceded to Britain; extraterritoriality; unequal treaties → China's century of humiliation

Concept: The Scramble for Africa: Berlin Conference (1884-85) established rules; 90% of Africa colonized by 1914; Hausa/Fulani/Yoruba/Zulu kingdoms dismantled; borders ignored ethnicities.
Notes: Scramble for Africa: Berlin Conference 1884-85 rules; 90% Africa colonized by 1914; kingdoms dismantled; arbitrary borders

Concept: The Nixon Doctrine (1969): US allies must handle own conventional defense; US provides nuclear umbrella and aid but not troops; context of Vietnam failure.
Notes: Nixon Doctrine 1969: allies defend themselves conventionally; US provides nuclear umbrella + aid but no troops; post-Vietnam

Concept: Neorealism (Waltz): structure of international system (anarchy + distribution of capabilities) shapes state behavior; balance-of-power emerges from self-help; units are states.
Notes: neorealism (Waltz): system structure (anarchy + power distribution) shapes states; balance-of-power from self-help; units = states

Concept: The Responsibility to Protect (R2P): states have primary responsibility to protect citizens; if state fails, international community may intervene; endorsed 2005 UN Summit.
Notes: R2P: states primary duty to protect citizens; if they fail → intl community can intervene; endorsed 2005 UN Summit

Concept: The liberal peace theory: democracies rarely fight each other (democratic peace); interdependence reduces conflict; international institutions increase transparency and cooperation.
Notes: liberal peace: democracies rarely fight each other; ↑ interdependence → ↓ conflict; intl institutions → cooperation

Concept: Antonio Gramsci's hegemony: ruling class maintains power through cultural consensus, not just coercion; intellectuals serve to naturalize ruling ideology; counter-hegemony needed.
Notes: Gramsci hegemony: ruling class = cultural consensus + coercion; organic intellectuals naturalize ideology; counter-hegemony needed

Concept: The Orange Revolution (Ukraine 2004): Viktor Yanukovych election fraud → mass protests → re-run won by Yushchenko; precedent for Maidan 2014; Russia-West proxy conflict.
Notes: Orange Revolution Ukraine 2004: Yanukovych fraud → protests → Yushchenko wins re-run; precedent for Maidan 2014

Concept: The Bandung Conference (1955): 29 Asian and African nations; non-alignment movement; Nehru, Nasser, Sukarno; rejected Cold War blocs; five principles of peaceful coexistence.
Notes: Bandung 1955: 29 Asian+African nations; non-alignment; Nehru/Nasser/Sukarno; rejected Cold War blocs; 5 peaceful coexistence principles

Concept: The Marshall Plan and European integration: US aid conditioned on European cooperation → led to OEEC → later EEC (1957 Treaty of Rome) → EU; spillover functionalism.
Notes: Marshall Plan → OEEC → EEC (Treaty of Rome 1957) → EU; US conditionality → European integration; functionalist spillover

Concept: The Iranian Revolution (1979): Shah overthrown; Ayatollah Khomeini establishes Islamic Republic; US hostage crisis 444 days; export of revolution doctrine; permanent US-Iran hostility.
Notes: Iranian Revolution 1979: Shah overthrown → Khomeini + Islamic Republic; US hostage crisis 444 days; export revolution doctrine

Concept: The Thirty Years War (1618-48): religious → power-political conflict; Defenestration of Prague spark; Peace of Westphalia ended it; established state sovereignty norm.
Notes: Thirty Years War 1618-48: religious→political; Prague defenestration → Westphalia 1648 → state sovereignty norm established

Concept: The causes of World War I: alliance system (Triple Alliance vs Triple Entente), arms race, colonial rivalries, nationalism (especially Slavic), Austro-Hungarian imperial crisis, miscalculation.
Notes: WWI causes: alliance system + arms race + colonial rivalry + nationalism + A-H imperial crisis + miscalculation

Concept: Rational choice theory in political science: actors maximize utility given preferences and constraints; game theory models strategic interaction; Arrow's impossibility theorem limits collective choice.
Notes: rational choice: actors maximize utility; game theory = strategic interaction; Arrow's impossibility = collective choice paradox

Concept: The Velvet Revolution (1989): Czechoslovakia's peaceful transition from communist rule; student protest → general strike → Havel elected president; contrast with Tiananmen (1989).
Notes: Velvet Revolution 1989: peaceful Czechoslovakia transition; student protest → general strike → Havel president; ≠ Tiananmen

Concept: The post-colonial state: weak institutions, arbitrary borders, neo-patrimonial rule, resource curse, ethnic fragmentation; Mamdani: citizen vs. subject distinction from colonial rule.
Notes: post-colonial state: weak institutions + arbitrary borders + neo-patrimonialism + resource curse; Mamdani: citizen/subject divide

Concept: The Cuban Revolution (1959): Fidel Castro overthrew Batista; US Bay of Pigs failure (1961); Cuban Missile Crisis (1962); Castro aligned w/ USSR; 50+ years of US embargo.
Notes: Cuban Revolution 1959: Castro overthrows Batista; Bay of Pigs 1961 (US failure); Missile Crisis 1962; USSR alignment; US embargo 50+ yrs

Concept: The Bretton Woods system (1944): fixed exchange rates; dollar pegged to gold ($35/oz); IMF for balance of payments; World Bank for reconstruction; Nixon ended it 1971.
Notes: Bretton Woods 1944: fixed FX; USD pegged to gold ($35/oz); IMF + World Bank; Nixon ended 1971 (closed gold window)

Concept: The Long Peace (Gaddis): absence of great power war since 1945; explained by nuclear deterrence, US hegemony, economic interdependence, democratic peace.
Notes: Long Peace (Gaddis): no great power war since 1945; causes: nuclear deterrence + US hegemony + interdependence + democratic peace

Concept: The Sykes-Picot Agreement (1916): Britain and France secretly divided Ottoman Middle East; borders ignored tribal/ethnic/sectarian realities; foundation of modern Middle East conflicts.
Notes: Sykes-Picot 1916: secret Anglo-French division of Ottoman ME; ignored tribal/ethnic/sectarian realities; → modern ME conflicts

Concept: The European Union's democratic deficit: European Parliament has limited power; Council/Commission less accountable; technocratic governance; national parliaments bypassed.
Notes: EU democratic deficit: EP limited power; Council/Commission unaccountable; technocratic; national parliaments bypassed

Concept: The Washington Consensus: 10 policy prescriptions (fiscal discipline, liberalization, privatization); pushed by IMF/World Bank for developing countries; backlash from 1990s crises.
Notes: Washington Consensus: fiscal discipline+liberalization+privatization (10 policies); IMF/WB push; backlash from 1990s crises

Concept: The rise of China: Deng Xiaoping's reforms (1978) → SEZs → export-led growth; WTO accession (2001); GDP from $149B (1978) to $18T (2023); military modernization.
Notes: China rise: Deng reforms 1978 → SEZs + export growth; WTO 2001; GDP $149B→$18T; military modernization

Concept: Max Weber's Protestant Ethic: Calvinist predestination → worldly asceticism → capital accumulation → capitalism; inner-worldly asceticism as cultural driver.
Notes: Weber Protestant Ethic: Calvinist predestination → worldly asceticism → capital accumulation → capitalism; cultural explanation

Concept: Decolonization and partition: British India (1947) produced Hindu-Muslim violence; British Palestine (1948) produced Arab-Israeli conflict; Ireland (1921) → sectarian conflict; common pattern.
Notes: British partition pattern: India 1947 (Hindu-Muslim), Palestine 1948 (Arab-Israeli), Ireland 1921 (sectarian); partition → violence

Concept: Antibiotics: beta-lactams (penicillins, cephalosporins) inhibit cell wall synthesis; aminoglycosides inhibit 30S ribosome; fluoroquinolones inhibit DNA gyrase; macrolides inhibit 50S.
Notes: antibiotics: beta-lactams → ↓ cell wall; aminoglycosides → 30S ribosome; fluoroquinolones → DNA gyrase; macrolides → 50S ribosome

Concept: The renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system: low blood pressure → renin released → angiotensinogen → Ang I → ACE → Ang II → vasoconstriction + aldosterone → Na+ retention → ↑ BP.
Notes: RAAS: ↓ BP → renin → angiotensinogen → AngI → ACE → AngII → vasoconstriction + aldosterone → ↑ Na+ → ↑ BP

Concept: Septic shock: distributive shock from vasodilation + maldistribution of flow; warm shock (vasodilation, ↑ CO initially) → cold shock (↓ CO, vasoconstriction); sepsis-3 definition.
Notes: septic shock: distributive; warm (vasodilated, ↑CO) → cold (↓CO, vasoconstricted); sepsis-3: organ dysfunction + vasopressors + lactate>2

Concept: Myocardial infarction: complete occlusion → ST elevation (STEMI) → transmural ischemia; partial occlusion → NSTEMI; troponin elevated both; ECG changes localize territory.
Notes: MI: complete occlusion → STEMI (transmural); partial → NSTEMI; troponin ↑ both; ECG localizes territory (II/III/aVF = inferior; V1-V4 = anterior)

Concept: Drug metabolism: CYP450 enzymes in liver (Phase I = oxidation/reduction/hydrolysis); Phase II = conjugation (glucuronidation, sulfation); first-pass effect reduces bioavailability.
Notes: drug metabolism: Phase I (CYP450: oxidation/reduction/hydrolysis); Phase II (conjugation: glucuronidation/sulfation); first-pass → ↓ bioavailability

Concept: The opioid system: mu (analgesic, euphoric, respiratory depression); kappa (dysphoric, spinal analgesia); delta (mood); naloxone reverses mu effects; tolerance via receptor downregulation.
Notes: opioids: mu (analgesia+euphoria+respiratory depression); kappa (dysphoria+spinal analgesia); delta (mood); naloxone = mu antagonist; tolerance → receptor downregulation

Concept: Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA): absolute insulin deficiency → ↑ glucagon → lipolysis + ketogenesis + gluconeogenesis → hyperglycemia + anion-gap acidosis + dehydration.
Notes: DKA: absolute insulin deficiency → ↑ glucagon → lipolysis+ketogenesis+gluconeogenesis → hyperglycemia + anion-gap acidosis + dehydration

Concept: Heart failure: reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF, systolic, EF<40%) vs. preserved EF (HFpEF, diastolic, EF≥50%); Frank-Starling: ↑ preload → ↑ contractility until plateau.
Notes: heart failure: HFrEF (systolic, EF<40%) vs. HFpEF (diastolic, EF≥50%); Frank-Starling: ↑ preload → ↑ contractility until plateau

Concept: Pulmonary embolism: DVT → clot in pulmonary artery → ↑ dead space + ↓ oxygenation; Wells score + D-dimer; CTPA gold standard; anticoagulation; thrombolysis for massive PE.
Notes: PE: DVT → pulmonary artery clot → ↑ dead space + ↓ O2; Wells+D-dimer screen; CTPA gold standard; anticoagulate; thrombolyse if massive

Concept: Stroke: ischemic (80%, thrombotic or embolic) vs. hemorrhagic (20%); tPA within 4.5 hr for ischemic; NIH stroke scale; FAST (face, arm, speech, time).
Notes: stroke: ischemic 80% (thrombotic/embolic) vs. hemorrhagic 20%; tPA within 4.5 hr (ischemic); FAST; NIH stroke scale

Concept: The renin-angiotensin blockade: ACE inhibitors (end in -pril) block Ang I→Ang II; ARBs (-sartan) block AT1 receptor; both beneficial in HF, HTN, CKD; ACE-I cause cough (bradykinin).
Notes: ACEi (pril): block AngI→AngII; ARBs (sartan): block AT1R; both = HTN+HF+CKD; ACEi → cough (bradykinin ↑)

Concept: COPD: chronic obstructive = emphysema (airspace enlargement, ↓ elastic recoil) + chronic bronchitis (productive cough ≥3 mo/2 yr); FEV1/FVC <0.7 post-bronchodilator.
